automated\n// reaction (e.g. re-training, escalation to human-in-the-loop, …).\ntype DriftEvent struct {\n\tModelID string\n\tFeature string\n\tPSI     float64\n\tTime    time.Time\n}\n\n// DriftHandler is the pluggable strategy that defines how the monitor\n// reacts to drift events.\ntype DriftHandler interface {\n\tOnDrift(ctx context.Context, ev DriftEvent) error\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Construction helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// NewDriftMonitor instantiates a fully-wired monitor with sane defaults.\n// The caller must invoke Start() to begin processing.\nfunc NewDriftMonitor(parentCtx context.Context, cfg Config, handler DriftHandler) (*DriftMonitor, error) {\n\tif len(cfg.Brokers) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"driftmonitor: empty broker list\")\n\t}\n\tif cfg.Topic == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"driftmonitor: empty topic\")\n\t}\n\tif handler == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"driftmonitor: nil handler\")\n\t}\n\tr := kafka.NewReader(kafka.ReaderConfig{\n\t\tBrokers:   cfg.Brokers,\n\t\tTopic:     cfg.Topic,\n\t\tGroupID:   cfg.GroupID,\n\t\tMinBytes:  cfg.MinBytes,\n\t\tMaxBytes:  cfg.MaxBytes,\n\t\tMaxWait:   cfg.MaxWait,\n\t\tPartition: 0,\n\t})\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(parentCtx)\n\n\treturn &DriftMonitor{\n\t\tcfg:    cfg,\n\t\treader: r,\n\t\thandler: handler,\n\t\tctx:    ctx,\n\t\tcancel: cancel,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// Start begins consumption on a dedicated goroutine.\nfunc (m *DriftMonitor) Start() {\n\tm.startOnce.Do(func() {\n\t\tlogrus.Infof(\"driftmonitor: starting (topic=%s group=%s)\", m.cfg.Topic, m.cfg.GroupID)\n\t\tm.wg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo m.loop()\n\t})\n}\n\n// Stop gracefully shuts down the monitor and waits for inflight\n// processing to finish (bounded by Context deadline/cancel).\nfunc (m *DriftMonitor) Stop() {\n\tm.stopOnce.Do(func() {\n\t\tlogrus.Info(\"driftmonitor: stopping\")\n\t\tm.cancel()\n\t\tm.wg.Wait()\n\t\t_ = m.reader.Close()\n\t})\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Event loop\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunc (m *DriftMonitor) loop() {\n\tdefer m.wg.Done()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tmsg, err := m.reader.ReadMessage(m.ctx)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\n\t\t\t\treturn // normal shutdown\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlogrus.WithError(err).Warn(\"driftmonitor: read error\")\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar metric DriftMetric\n\t\tif err = json.Unmarshal(msg.Value, &metric); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlogrus.WithError(err).Warn(\"driftmonitor: invalid json payload\")\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Sanity guard\n\t\tif metric.ModelID == \"\" || metric.Feature == \"\" {\n\t\t\tlogrus.Warn(\"driftmonitor: missing model/feature identifier\")\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Evaluate drift\n\t\tif metric.PSI >= m.cfg.PSIThreshold {\n\t\t\tev := DriftEvent{\n\t\t\t\tModelID: metric.ModelID,\n\t\t\t\tFeature: metric.Feature,\n\t\t\t\tPSI:     metric.PSI,\n\t\t\t\tTime:    metric.Timestamp,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlogrus.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\n\t\t\t\t\"model\":   ev.ModelID,\n\t\t\t\t\"feature\": ev.Feature,\n\t\t\t\t\"psi\":     fmt.Sprintf(\"%.4f\", ev.PSI),\n\t\t\t}).Warn(\"driftmonitor: drift detected\")\n\n\t\t\t// Forward to downstream handler\n\t\t\tif err := m.handler.OnDrift(m.ctx, ev); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlogrus.WithError(err).Error(\"driftmonitor: handler error\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Default Handler — HTTP trainer client\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// TrainerClient is a minimal REST client that wraps the model-trainer\n// service. In production this might be gRPC, but HTTP keeps the sample\n// self-contained.\ntype TrainerClient interface {\n\t// TriggerRetrain requests an immediate (ad-hoc) training job and\n\t// returns the job identifier on success.\n\tTriggerRetrain(ctx context.Context, modelID, reason string) (string, error)\n}\n\n// HTTPTrainerClient is a best-effort implementation backed by Go's\n// stdlib HTTP stack.\ntype HTTPTrainerClient struct {\n\tEndpoint   string            // e.g. http://trainer.svc.cluster.local:8080\n\tHTTPClient *http.Client      // injected for testability\n\tHeaders    map[string]string // optional static headers (auth, trace, …)\n}\n\n// TriggerRetrain fires a POST /v1/train endpoint with a JSON body.\nfunc (c *HTTPTrainerClient) TriggerRetrain(ctx context.Context, modelID, reason string) (string, error) {\n\tif c.Endpoint == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\", errors.New(\"trainerclient: empty endpoint\")\n\t}\n\tbody := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"model_id\": modelID,\n\t\t\"reason\":   reason,\n\t}\n\tbuf, _ := json.Marshal(body) // cannot fail\n\treq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, c.Endpoint+\"/v1/train\", strings.NewReader(string(buf)))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\treq.Header.Set(\"content-type\", \"application/json\")\n\tfor k, v := range c.Headers {\n\t\treq.Header.Set(k, v)\n\t}\n\tclient := c.HTTPClient\n\tif client == nil {\n\t\tclient = http.DefaultClient\n\t}\n\tresp, err := client.Do(req)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\tif resp.StatusCode >= 300 {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"trainerclient: unexpected HTTP %d\", resp.StatusCode)\n\t}\n\tvar out struct {\n\t\tJobID string `json:\"job_id\"`\n\t}\n\tif err = json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&out); err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\tif out.JobID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\", errors.New(\"trainerclient: empty job_id\")\n\t}\n\treturn out.JobID, nil\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Concrete DriftHandler implementation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// RetrainHandler is a DriftHandler that directly calls the trainer\n// service when drift occurs.\ntype RetrainHandler struct {\n\tclient TrainerClient\n}\n\n// NewRetrainHandler builds a handler with the supplied client.\nfunc NewRetrainHandler(client TrainerClient) *RetrainHandler {\n\treturn &RetrainHandler{client: client}\n}\n\n// OnDrift implements the DriftHandler interface.\nfunc (h *RetrainHandler) OnDrift(ctx context.Context, ev DriftEvent) error {\n\treason := fmt.Sprintf(\"psi=%.4f feature=%s at %s\", ev.PSI, ev.Feature, ev.Time.Format(time.RFC3339))\n\tjobID, err := h.client.TriggerRetrain(ctx, ev.ModelID, reason)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tlogrus.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\n\t\t\"model\":  ev.ModelID,\n\t\t\"job_id\": jobID,\n\t}).Info(\"driftmonitor: auto-retraining kicked off\")\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Bootstrap helper for CLI or tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// RunDriftMonitorFromEnv is a convenience entry-point that wires the\n// monitor using environment variables. It is suitable for docker-ised\n// sidecars or local dev.\n//\n//   ECHOPULSE_BROKERS=broker1:9092,broker2:9092\n//   ECHOPULSE_MONITOR_TOPIC=feature_drift\n//   ECHOPULSE_GROUP_ID=ml_drift_monitor\n//   ECHOPULSE_PSI_THRESHOLD=0.2\n//\n// A Ctrl-C (SIGINT) will trigger graceful shutdown.\nfunc RunDriftMonitorFromEnv() error {\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// Handle Ctrl-C.\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tsig := make(chan os.Signal, 1)\n\t\tsignal.Notify(sig, os.Interrupt)\n\t\t<-sig\n\t\tcancel()\n\t}()\n\n\tbrokers := strings.Split(os.Getenv(\"ECHOPULSE_BROKERS\"), \",\")\n\ttopic := getenvDefault(\"ECHOPULSE_MONITOR_TOPIC\", \"feature_drift\")\n\tgroup := getenvDefault(\"ECHOPULSE_GROUP_ID\", \"ml_drift_monitor\")\n\n\tpsiThreshold, _ := strconv.ParseFloat(getenvDefault(\"ECHOPULSE_PSI_THRESHOLD\", \"0.2\"), 64)\n\n\tcfg := Config{\n\t\tBrokers:      brokers,\n\t\tTopic:        topic,\n\t\tGroupID:      group,\n\t\tMinBytes:     1e4,           // 10KiB\n\t\tMaxBytes:     5e6,           // 5MiB\n\t\tMaxWait:      200 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tPSIThreshold: psiThreshold,\n\t}\n\n\thandler := NewRetrainHandler(&HTTPTrainerClient{\n\t\tEndpoint: getenvDefault(\"ECHOPULSE_TRAINER_ENDPOINT\", \"http://trainer:8080\"),\n\t\tHeaders:  map[string]string{\"X-Requested-By\": \"echopulse-drift-monitor\"},\n\t})\n\n\tmonitor, err := NewDriftMonitor(ctx, cfg, handler)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tmonitor.Start()\n\t<-ctx.Done()\n\tmonitor.Stop()\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Utilities\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunc getenvDefault(key, def string) string {\n\tval := os.Getenv(key)\n\tif val == \"\" {\n\t\treturn def\n\t}\n\treturn val\n}\n\n// signal is imported lazily via side-effect for Unix platforms.\nvar signal struct {\n\tNotify func(chan<- os.Signal, ...os.Signal)\n}\n\n// init resolves the platform-specific signal package (stubbed for\n// Windows where syscall.SIGINT isn't defined).\nfunc init() {\n\tvar (\n\t\tonce sync.Once\n\t\terr  error\n\t)\n\tonce.Do(func() {\n\t\tsignal.Notify, err = loadSignalNotify()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// If signal.Notify cannot be loaded the monitor still works,\n\t\t\t// but won't respond to Ctrl-C. Log and continue.\n\t\t\tlogrus.WithError(err).Warn(\"driftmonitor: signal Notify unavailable\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// loadSignalNotify attempts to obtain the os/signal.Notify symbol\n// via reflection so the code compiles on all platforms without a\n// direct import (which would break on JS/WASM).\nfunc loadSignalNotify() (func(chan<- os.Signal, ...os.Signal), error) {\n\tpkg, err := importPkg(\"os/signal\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tsym, ok := pkg.Lookup(\"Notify\")\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"symbol Notify not found\")\n\t}\n\tfn, ok := sym.Interface().(func(chan<- os.Signal, ...os.Signal))\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"Notify has unexpected type\")\n\t}\n\treturn fn, nil\n}\n\n// importPkg loads a package by path at runtime. It relies on the\n// plugin mechanism which is unavailable on Windows/JS, but the code\n// remains safe thanks to graceful degradation.\nfunc importPkg(path string) (*plugin.Plugin, error) {\n\treturn nil, errors.New(\"dynamic import not supported in this build\")\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Mock helpers (used in unit-tests)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// NewTestHandler returns a no-op DriftHandler that records received\n// events into the supplied slice for assertions in tests.\nfunc NewTestHandler(out *[]DriftEvent) DriftHandler {\n\tvar mu sync.Mutex\n\treturn DriftHandlerFunc(func(_ context.Context, ev DriftEvent) error {\n\t\tmu.Lock()\n\t\tdefer mu.Unlock()\n\t\t*out = append(*out, ev)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n}\n\n// DriftHandlerFunc is a functional adapter to build handlers inline.\ntype DriftHandlerFunc func(ctx context.Context, ev DriftEvent) error\n\n// OnDrift implements the DriftHandler interface.\nfunc (f DriftHandlerFunc) OnDrift(ctx context.Context, ev DriftEvent) error { return f(ctx, ev) }\n```", "numLines": 421, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 421}}

    43→

    45→// fanned-out to downstream services/dashboards.
    46→type CommunityHealthEvent struct {

    52→
    53→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

    55→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    56→
    57→// Config is resolved from environment variables so the binary can be
    58→// containerised and scheduled without additional configuration management.
    59→type Config struct {

    62→	OutgoingTopic  string

    67→
    68→// NewConfigFromEnv reads & validates configuration from environment variables.
    69→func NewConfigFromEnv() (Config, error) {
    70→	cfg := Config{
    71→		Brokers:        strings.Split(os.Getenv("EP_BROKERS"), ","),
    72→		IncomingTopic:  getenvDefault("EP_IN_TOPIC", "social.events"),
    73→		OutgoingTopic:  getenvDefault("EP_OUT_TOPIC", "community.health"),
    74→		ConsumerGroup:  getenvDefault("EP_GROUP_ID", "sentiment-aggregator"),
    75→		Alpha:          getenvFloatDefault("EP_ALPHA", 0.3),
    76→		MetricsAddress: getenvDefault("EP_METRICS_ADDR", ":2112"),
    77→	}
    78→
    79→	if len(cfg.Brokers) == 0 || cfg.Brokers[0] == "" {
    80→		return Config{}, errors.New("EP_BROKERS cannot be empty")
    81→	}
    82→	if cfg.Alpha <= 0.0 || cfg.Alpha >= 1.0 {
    83→		return Config{}, errors.New("EP_ALPHA must be in (0,1)")
    84→	}
    85→	return cfg, nil
    86→}

    91→	if v := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(key)); v != "" {

   112→// SentimentAggregator consumes raw SocialEvents, maintains a per-channel

   139→	prodCfg := sarama.NewConfig()

   155→	consumer, err := sarama.NewConsumerGroup(cfg.Brokers, cfg.ConsumerGroup, consCfg)
   156→	if err != nil {
   157→		_ = producer.Close()
   158→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create consumer group: %w", err)
   159→	}
   160→
   161→	// Prometheus metrics --------------------------------------------------------------
   162→	registry := prometheus.DefaultRegisterer
   163→	metricScore := prometheus.NewGaugeVec(
   164→		prometheus.GaugeOpts{
   165→			Namespace: "echopulse",
   166→			Subsystem: "sentiment_aggregator",
   167→			Name:      "ewma_score",
   168→			Help:      "Smoothed sentiment score per channel",
   169→		},
   170→		[]string{"channel_id"},
   171→	)
   172→	registry.MustRegister(metricScore)
   173→
   174→	metricMessages := prometheus.NewCounter(
   175→		prometheus.CounterOpts{
   176→			Namespace: "echopulse",
   177→			Subsystem: "sentiment_aggregator",
   178→			Name:      "messages_ingested_total",
   179→			Help:      "Number of SocialEvents processed",
   180→		},
   181→	)
   182→	registry.MustRegister(metricMessages)
   183→
   184→	metricPublished := prometheus.NewCounter(
   185→		prometheus.CounterOpts{
   186→			Namespace: "echopulse",
   187→			Subsystem: "sentiment_aggregator",
   188→			Name:      "messages_published_total",
   189→			Help:      "Number of CommunityHealthEvents published",
   190→		},
   191→	)
   192→	registry.MustRegister(metricPublished)
   193→
   194→	return &SentimentAggregator{
   195→		cfg:            cfg,
   196→		producer:       producer,
   197→		consumer:       consumer,
   198→		state:          make(map[string]*channelState),
   199→		metricScore:    metricScore,
   200→		metricMessages: metricMessages,
   201→		metricPublished: metricPublished,
   202→	}, nil
   203→}
   204→
   205→// Start begins consuming messages and blocks until context cancellation or fatal error.
   206→func (sa *SentimentAggregator) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
   207→	// Expose /metrics -----------------------------------------------------------
   208→	go func() {
   209→		http.Handle("/metrics", promhttp.Handler())
   210→		if err := http.ListenAndServe(sa.cfg.MetricsAddress, nil); err != nil {
   211→			log.Printf("metrics server exited: %v", err)
   212→		}
   213→	}()
   214→
   215→	wg := &sync.WaitGroup{}
   216→	wg.Add(1)
   217→
   218→	go func() {
   219→		defer wg.Done()
   220→		backoffCfg := backoff.NewExponentialBackOff()
   221→		for {
   222→			// Consume() is blocking, returning only on error or ctx cancel.
   223→			if err := sa.consumer.Consume(ctx, []string{sa.cfg.IncomingTopic}, &consumerGroupHandler{agg: sa}); err != nil {
   224→				// Transient errors (e.g. broker down) are back-off retried.
   225→				if ctx.Err() != nil {
   226→					// context cancelled -> graceful shutdown
   227→					return
   228→				}
   229→				next := backoffCfg.NextBackOff()
   230→				log.Printf("consume error: %v; retrying in %s", err, next)
   231→				select {
   232→				case <-time.After(next):
   233→				case <-ctx.Done():
   234→					return
   235→				}
   236→				continue
   237→			}
   238→			// Reached here if Consume exited without error -> context cancelled.
   239→			return
   240→		}
   241→	}()
   242→
   243→	<-ctx.Done() // Wait for caller.
   244→	wg.Wait()
   245→	return nil
   246→}
   247→
   248→// Stop gracefully closes the Kafka clients.
   249→func (sa *SentimentAggregator) Stop() {
   250→	_ = sa.consumer.Close()
   251→	_ = sa.producer.Close()
   252→}
   253→
   254→// processSocialEvent updates EWMA state and publishes a CommunityHealthEvent.
   255→func (sa *SentimentAggregator) processSocialEvent(ev *SocialEvent) error {
   256→	sa.metricMessages.Inc()
   257→
   258→	sa.stateMu.Lock()
   259→	st, ok := sa.state[ev.ChannelID]
   260→	if !ok {
   261→		st = &channelState{}
   262→		sa.state[ev.ChannelID] = st
   263→	}
   264→	// EWMA update: S_t = α*x_t + (1-α)*S_{t-1}
   265→	st.Score = sa.cfg.Alpha*ev.Sentiment + (1.0-sa.cfg.Alpha)*st.Score
   266→	sa.stateMu.Unlock()
   267→
   268→	sa.metricScore.WithLabelValues(ev.ChannelID).Set(st.Score)
   269→
   270→	health := CommunityHealthEvent{
   271→		ChannelID: ev.ChannelID,
   272→		Score:     st.Score,
   273→		Window:    fmt.Sprintf("EWMA(α=%.2f)", sa.cfg.Alpha),
   274→		CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
   275→	}
   276→
   277→	payload, err := json.Marshal(health)
   278→	if err != nil {
   279→		return fmt.Errorf("marshal health event: %w", err)
   280→	}
   281→
   282→	msg := &sarama.ProducerMessage{
   283→		Topic: sa.cfg.OutgoingTopic,
   284→		Value: sarama.ByteEncoder(payload),
   285→		Key:   sarama.StringEncoder(ev.ChannelID),
   286→	}
   287→
   288→	_, _, err = sa.producer.SendMessage(msg)
   289→	if err != nil {
   290→		return fmt.Errorf("publish health event: %w", err)
   291→	}
   292→	sa.metricPublished.Inc()
   293→	return nil
   294→}
   295→
   296→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   297→// Sarama ConsumerGroup handler implementation
   298→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   299→
   300→type consumerGroupHandler struct {
   301→	agg *SentimentAggregator
   302→}
   303→
   304→func (h *consumerGroupHandler) Setup(_ sarama.ConsumerGroupSession) error   { return nil }
   305→func (h *consumerGroupHandler) Cleanup(_ sarama.ConsumerGroupSession) error { return nil }
   306→
   307→func (h *consumerGroupHandler) ConsumeClaim(session sarama.ConsumerGroupSession, claim sarama.ConsumerGroupClaim) error {
   308→	for msg := range claim.Messages() {
   309→		var ev SocialEvent
   310→		if err := json.Unmarshal(msg.Value, &ev); err != nil {
   311→			log.Printf("invalid SocialEvent: %v", err)
   312→			continue // Skip but mark offset to avoid poison pill loops.
   313→		}
   314→		if err := h.agg.processSocialEvent(&ev); err != nil {
   315→			log.Printf("process error (ack skipped): %v", err)
   316→			continue
   317→		}
   318→		session.MarkMessage(msg, "")
   319→	}
   320→	return nil
   321→}
   322→
   323→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   324→// Main (stand-alone service entry point)
   325→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   326→
   327→// For package reuse we keep main in the same file, guarded by build tags.
   328→//
   329→//go:build sentiment_aggregator
   330→// +build sentiment_aggregator
   331→
   332→func main() {
   333→	cfg, err := NewConfigFromEnv()
   334→	if err != nil {
   335→		log.Fatalf("invalid configuration: %v", err)
   336→	}
   337→
   338→	agg, err := NewSentimentAggregator(cfg)
   339→	if err != nil {
   340→		log.Fatalf("initialise aggregator: %v", err)
   341→	}
   342→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
   343→	defer cancel()
   344→
   345→	// Respond to SIGINT/SIGTERM for graceful shutdown.
   346→	go handleSignals(cancel)
   347→
   348→	log.Println("Sentiment Aggregator starting …")
   349→	if err := agg.Start(ctx); err != nil {
   350→		log.Fatalf("aggregator stopped with error: %v", err)
   351→	}
   352→	agg.Stop()
   353→	log.Println("Sentiment Aggregator shut down gracefully.")
   354→}
   355→
   356→// handleSignals listens for termination signals and cancels the context.
   357→func handleSignals(cancel context.CancelFunc) {
   358→	sigCh := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
   359→	// Refresh to import the package only on this build tag.
   360→	//nolint:staticcheck
   361→	signal.Notify(sigCh, os.Interrupt, os.Kill)
   362→	<-sigCh
   363→	cancel()
   364→}

1→package monitoring
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"errors"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"sync"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"go.uber.org/zap"
    12→	"gonum.org/v1/gonum/stat/distuv"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// ========= Interfaces =======================================================
    16→
    17→// BusConsumer defines the minimal contract for a message-bus consumer.
    18→// It intentionally mirrors the subset of the sarama.ConsumerGroup interface
    19→// that we actually need, which makes the implementation easily swappable
    20→// for Kafka, NATS JetStream, in-memory mocks, etc.
    21→type BusConsumer interface {
    22→	Consume(ctx context.Context, topics []string, handler MessageHandler) error
    23→	Close() error
    24→}
    25→
    26→// MessageHandler is called by the BusConsumer for every new message.
    27→type MessageHandler func(ctx context.Context, raw []byte, ts time.Time) error
    28→
    29→// FeatureStore provides access to persistent reference distributions that were
    30→// calculated during model training/validation.  This abstraction hides the
    31→// implementation details (SQL, S3, in-memory, …).
    32→type FeatureStore interface {
    33→	GetReferenceDistribution(model, feature string) (map[string]float64, error)
    34→}
    35→
    36→// EventEmitter delivers DriftEvents to downstream services (observability
    37→// dashboards, alerting brokers, retraining pipelines, …).
    38→type EventEmitter interface {
    39→	EmitDriftEvent(ctx context.Context, evt DriftEvent) error
    40→}
    41→
    42→// ========= Public Types =====================================================
    43→
    44→// DriftEvent is published whenever the statistical divergence between the
    45→// observed and reference distributions crosses the configured alpha.
    46→// Downstream consumers may decide to escalate, trigger retraining, etc.
    47→type DriftEvent struct {
    48→	Model               string             `json:"model"`
    49→	Feature             string             `json:"feature"`
    50→	WindowStart         time.Time          `json:"window_start"`
    51→	WindowEnd           time.Time          `json:"window_end"`
    52→	ObservedCounts      map[string]int64   `json:"observed_counts"`
    53→	ReferenceProbs      map[string]float64 `json:"reference_probs"`
    54→	ChiSquareStatistic  float64            `json:"chi_square_statistic"`
    55→	PValue              float64            `json:"p_value"`
    56→	SignificanceWarning bool               `json:"significance_warning"`
    57→}
    58→
    59→// DriftMonitorConfig drives the runtime behavior of the monitor.
    60→type DriftMonitorConfig struct {
    61→	ModelName   string        // Name of the model we are monitoring
    62→	Feature     string        // Name of the categorical output feature (e.g. "sentiment")
    63→	Topic       string        // Kafka/NATS topic carrying predictions
    64→	WindowSize  time.Duration // Sliding window length
    65→	BucketSize  time.Duration // Granularity of time buckets inside the window
    66→	MinSamples  int           // Minimum n to calculate drift
    67→	Alpha       float64       // Significance level for chi-square test (e.g. 0.05)
    68→	Logger      *zap.Logger   // Optional; falls back to zap.NewNop()
    69→}
    70→
    71→// ========= Implementation ===================================================
    72→
    73→// DriftMonitor consumes model predictions in real time, aggregates them into a
    74→// sliding window, compares the observed class distribution with the reference
    75→// distribution fetched from the FeatureStore, and emits DriftEvents whenever
    76→// statistical drift is detected with significance < alpha.
    77→type DriftMonitor struct {
    78→	cfg          DriftMonitorConfig
    79→	bus          BusConsumer
    80→	fs           FeatureStore
    81→	emitter      EventEmitter
    82→	mu           sync.RWMutex                   // protects buckets
    83→	buckets      map[int64]map[string]int64     // epoch bucket -> class -> count
    84→	totalPerBuck map[int64]int64                // epoch bucket -> total count
    85→	logger       *zap.Logger
    86→}
    87→
    88→// NewDriftMonitor wires together a new monitor instance.
    89→func NewDriftMonitor(cfg DriftMonitorConfig, bus BusConsumer, fs FeatureStore, emitter EventEmitter) (*DriftMonitor, error) {
    90→	if cfg.WindowSize <= 0 || cfg.BucketSize <= 0 {
    91→		return nil, errors.New("window and bucket durations must be > 0")
    92→	}
    93→	if cfg.WindowSize%cfg.BucketSize != 0 {
    94→		return nil, errors.New("window size must be a multiple of bucket size")
    95→	}
    96→	if cfg.Alpha <= 0 || cfg.Alpha >= 1 {
    97→		return nil, errors.New("alpha must be in (0,1)")
    98→	}
    99→	if cfg.Logger == nil {
   100→		cfg.Logger = zap.NewNop()
   101→	}
   102→
   103→	return &DriftMonitor{
   104→		cfg:          cfg,
   105→		bus:          bus,
   106→		fs:           fs,
   107→		emitter:      emitter,
   108→		buckets:      make(map[int64]map[string]int64),
   109→		totalPerBuck: make(map[int64]int64),
   110→		logger:       cfg.Logger,
   111→	}, nil
   112→}
   113→
   114→// Start blocks until ctx is canceled or the BusConsumer stops with error.
   115→func (dm *DriftMonitor) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
   116→	dm.logger.Info("starting drift monitor",
   117→		zap.String("model", dm.cfg.ModelName),
   118→		zap.String("feature", dm.cfg.Feature),
   119→		zap.String("topic", dm.cfg.Topic))
   120→
   121→	// Start periodic evaluator.
   122→	go dm.evaluationLoop(ctx)
   123→
   124→	// Attach consumer handler.
   125→	handler := func(c context.Context, raw []byte, ts time.Time) error {
   126→		return dm.handleMessage(raw, ts)
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	// This blocks until ctx.Done() or an error occurs.
   130→	if err := dm.bus.Consume(ctx, []string{dm.cfg.Topic}, handler); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   131→		return fmt.Errorf("bus consume failed: %w", err)
   132→	}
   133→	return nil
   134→}
   135→
   136→func (dm *DriftMonitor) handleMessage(raw []byte, ts time.Time) error {
   137→	var envelope struct {
   138→		Sentiment string `json:"sentiment"` // e.g., "positive", "neutral", "negative"
   139→	}
   140→	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &envelope); err != nil {
   141→		dm.logger.Warn("failed to unmarshal prediction", zap.Error(err))
   142→		return nil // swallow to keep pipeline alive
   143→	}
   144→	if envelope.Sentiment == "" {
   145→		return nil
   146→	}
   147→
   148→	bucket := ts.Unix() / int64(dm.cfg.BucketSize.Seconds())
   149→
   150→	dm.mu.Lock()
   151→	defer dm.mu.Unlock()
   152→
   153→	if _, ok := dm.buckets[bucket]; !ok {
   154→		dm.buckets[bucket] = make(map[string]int64)
   155→	}
   156→	dm.buckets[bucket][envelope.Sentiment]++
   157→	dm.totalPerBuck[bucket]++
   158→	return nil
   159→}
   160→
   161→// evaluationLoop runs a ticker that fires every BucketSize, sliding the window
   162→// and computing the chi-square drift metric.
   163→func (dm *DriftMonitor) evaluationLoop(ctx context.Context) {
   164→	ticker := time.NewTicker(dm.cfg.BucketSize)
   165→	defer ticker.Stop()
   166→
   167→	for {
   168→		select {
   169→		case <-ticker.C:
   170→			if err := dm.evaluateAndEmit(ctx); err != nil {
   171→				dm.logger.Error("evaluation failed", zap.Error(err))
   172→			}
   173→		case <-ctx.Done():
   174→			return
   175→		}
   176→	}
   177→}
   178→
   179→func (dm *DriftMonitor) evaluateAndEmit(ctx context.Context) error {
   180→	// Determine window range
   181→	end := time.Now()
   182→	start := end.Add(-dm.cfg.WindowSize)
   183→
   184→	startBucket := start.Unix() / int64(dm.cfg.BucketSize.Seconds())
   185→	endBucket := end.Unix() / int64(dm.cfg.BucketSize.Seconds())
   186→
   187→	// Aggregate counts inside window
   188→	dm.mu.RLock()
   189→	defer dm.mu.RUnlock()
   190→
   191→	observed := make(map[string]int64)
   192→	var total int64
   193→	for b := startBucket; b <= endBucket; b++ {
   194→		cnt := dm.buckets[b]
   195→		for class, n := range cnt {
   196→			observed[class] += n
   197→			total += n
   198→		}
   199→	}
   200→
   201→	if int(total) < dm.cfg.MinSamples {
   202→		dm.logger.Debug("skip drift eval, not enough samples", zap.Int64("total", total))
   203→		return nil
   204→	}
   205→
   206→	// Reference distribution
   207→	refProbs, err := dm.fs.GetReferenceDistribution(dm.cfg.ModelName, dm.cfg.Feature)
   208→	if err != nil {
   209→		return fmt.Errorf("feature store: %w", err)
   210→	}
   211→	if len(refProbs) == 0 {
   212→		return errors.New("reference distribution is empty")
   213→	}
   214→
   215→	chiSquare, pValue := chiSquareTest(observed, refProbs, total)
   216→
   217→	evt := DriftEvent{
   218→		Model:               dm.cfg.ModelName,
   219→		Feature:             dm.cfg.Feature,
   220→		WindowStart:         start,
   221→		WindowEnd:           end,
   222→		ObservedCounts:      observed,
   223→		ReferenceProbs:      refProbs,
   224→		ChiSquareStatistic:  chiSquare,
   225→		PValue:              pValue,
   226→		SignificanceWarning: pValue < dm.cfg.Alpha,
   227→	}
   228→
   229→	// Emit event
   230→	if err := dm.emitter.EmitDriftEvent(ctx, evt); err != nil {
   231→		return fmt.Errorf("emit: %w", err)
   232→	}
   233→
   234→	dm.logger.Debug("drift evaluated",
   235→		zap.Float64("chi_square", chiSquare),
   236→		zap.Float64("p_value", pValue),
   237→		zap.Bool("warning", evt.SignificanceWarning))
   238→
   239→	// Garbage collection of old buckets
   240→	go dm.gcOldBuckets(startBucket)
   241→
   242→	return nil
   243→}
   244→
   245→func (dm *DriftMonitor) gcOldBuckets(keepFrom int64) {
   246→	dm.mu.Lock()
   247→	defer dm.mu.Unlock()
   248→	for b := range dm.buckets {
   249→		if b < keepFrom {
   250→			delete(dm.buckets, b)
   251→			delete(dm.totalPerBuck, b)
   252→		}
   253→	}
   254→}
   255→
   256→// ========= Statistics Helpers ==============================================
   257→
   258→// chiSquareTest returns the chi-square statistic and two-tailed p-value.
   259→func chiSquareTest(observed map[string]int64, refProbs map[string]float64, total int64) (float64, float64) {
   260→	var chiSquare float64
   261→	var k int // degrees of freedom
   262→
   263→	for class, expProb := range refProbs {
   264→		exp := float64(total) * expProb
   265→		obs := float64(observed[class])
   266→		if exp == 0 {
   267→			// skip zero expected counts (not enough data for that class)
   268→			continue
   269→		}
   270→		diff := obs - exp
   271→		chiSquare += diff * diff / exp
   272→		k++
   273→	}
   274→
   275→	// If no degrees of freedom, p-value is 1
   276→	if k == 0 {
   277→		return 0, 1
   278→	}
   279→
   280→	dist := distuv.ChiSquared{K: float64(k - 1)}
   281→	pValue := 1 - dist.CDF(chiSquare)
   282→	return chiSquare, pValue
   283→}

     2→// File: src/module_31.go
     3→//
     4→// Package pipeline implements a high-throughput feature-extraction stage
     5→// that consumes canonical SocialEvents from NATS JetStream, enriches the
     6→// events with NLP-derived features (sentiment score, emoji density, etc.),
     7→// persists the results in the Feature Store, and publishes a downstream
     8→// FeatureVector message for follow-up stages (e.g. stance detection,
     9→// trend surfacing).  The component demonstrates idiomatic, production-
    10→// quality Go with attention to observability, back-pressure handling,
    11→// and graceful shutdown semantics.
    12→package pipeline
    13→
    14→import (
    15→	"context"
    16→	"encoding/json"
    17→	"errors"
    18→	"fmt"
    19→	"log"
    20→	"runtime/debug"
    21→	"strings"
    22→	"sync"
    23→	"time"
    24→
    25→	"github.com/go-redis/redis/v8"         // Feature cache
    26→	"github.com/google/uuid"               // Event correlation
    27→	"github.com/jonreiter/govader"         // Sentiment analysis
    28→	"github.com/nats-io/nats.go"           // Event bus
    29→	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel"             // Tracing
    30→	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute"   //
    31→	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace"       //
    32→	"google.golang.org/grpc"               // Feature-store client transport
    33→	pb "ml_nlp/proto/featurestore/v1"      // Feature-store protobuf stubs
    34→)
    35→
    36→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→// Domain Models
    38→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→
    40→// SocialEvent represents the canonical event produced by the ingestion tier.
    41→type SocialEvent struct {
    42→	ID          string            `json:"id"`
    43→	UserID      string            `json:"user_id"`
    44→	ChannelID   string            `json:"channel_id"`
    45→	Payload     string            `json:"payload"`      // Text, emoji, transcript
    46→	CreatedAt   time.Time         `json:"created_at"`   // Original timestamp
    47→	Metadata    map[string]string `json:"metadata"`     // Arbitrary k/v
    48→	ContentType string            `json:"content_type"` // "text", "emoji", "voice"
    49→}
    50→
    51→// FeatureVector is the downstream message created by this module.
    52→type FeatureVector struct {
    53→	EventID        string            `json:"event_id"`
    54→	UserID         string            `json:"user_id"`
    55→	ChannelID      string            `json:"channel_id"`
    56→	Sentiment      float64           `json:"sentiment"` // Normalized compound score
    57→	EmojiDensity   float64           `json:"emoji_density"`
    58→	WordCount      int               `json:"word_count"`
    59→	Language       string            `json:"language"`
    60→	GeneratedAt    time.Time         `json:"generated_at"`
    61→	AdditionalMeta map[string]string `json:"additional_meta"`
    62→}
    63→
    64→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    65→// Configuration
    66→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    67→
    68→// ExtractorConfig captures the dependencies and runtime knobs required by
    69→// FeatureExtractorService.
    70→type ExtractorConfig struct {
    71→	NATSURL              string        // NATS connection string
    72→	Subject              string        // NATS subject to subscribe to
    73→	FeatureVectorSubject string        // NATS subject to publish FeatureVector
    74→	ConsumerGroup        string        // JetStream durable consumer name
    75→	RedisURL             string        // Optional Redis cache ("" disables cache)
    76→	GRPCFeatureStoreURL  string        // Feature store endpoint (host:port)
    77→	Concurrency          int           // Worker pool size
    78→	Prefetch             int           // In-flight pull batch size
    79→	AckWait              time.Duration // Max time given to process a message
    80→}
    81→
    82→// Validate sanity-checks the configuration.
    83→func (c ExtractorConfig) Validate() error {
    84→	switch {
    85→	case c.NATSURL == "":
    86→		return errors.New("NATSURL is required")
    87→	case c.Subject == "":
    88→		return errors.New("Subject is required")
    89→	case c.FeatureVectorSubject == "":
    90→		return errors.New("FeatureVectorSubject is required")
    91→	case c.Concurrency <= 0:
    92→		return errors.New("Concurrency must be > 0")
    93→	case c.Prefetch <= 0 || c.Prefetch > 1024:
    94→		return errors.New("Prefetch must be between 1 and 1024")
    95→	case c.AckWait < 10*time.Second:
    96→		return errors.New("AckWait too small")
    97→	default:
    98→		return nil
    99→	}
   100→}
   101→
   102→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   103→// FeatureExtractorService
   104→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   105→
   106→// FeatureExtractorService coordinates subscription, worker pool, NLP, and I/O.
   107→type FeatureExtractorService struct {
   108→	cfg       ExtractorConfig
   109→	nc        *nats.Conn
   110→	js        nats.JetStreamContext
   111→	sub       *nats.Subscription
   112→	redis     *redis.Client
   113→	fsClient  pb.FeatureStoreServiceClient
   114→	tracer    trace.Tracer
   115→	analyzer  *govader.SentimentIntensityAnalyzer
   116→	wg        sync.WaitGroup
   117→	ctx       context.Context
   118→	cancel    context.CancelFunc
   119→	startOnce sync.Once
   120→	stopOnce  sync.Once
   121→}
   122→
   123→// NewFeatureExtractor constructs a ready-to-start service.
   124→func NewFeatureExtractor(cfg ExtractorConfig) (*FeatureExtractorService, error) {
   125→	if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil {
   126→		return nil, err
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	// NATS connection.
   130→	nc, err := nats.Connect(cfg.NATSURL,
   131→		nats.Name("EchoPulse/FeatureExtractor"),
   132→		nats.MaxReconnects(-1),
   133→	)

   157→		grpc.WithBlock(),
   158→		grpc.WithTimeout(5*time.Second),

   181→// Start boots the subscription and the worker pool.
   182→func (s *FeatureExtractorService) Start() error {

   190→// internal start logic separated for testability.
   191→func (s *FeatureExtractorService) start() error {

   194→		s.cfg.Subject,
   195→		s.cfg.ConsumerGroup,
   196→		nats.BindStream("SocialEvents"),
   197→		nats.AckWait(s.cfg.AckWait),

   239→// workerLoop polls JetStream for new messages and processes them serially.
   240→func (s *FeatureExtractorService) workerLoop(id int) {

   252→		default:
   253→		}

   269→				continue
   270→			}
   271→			_ = msg.Ack()
   272→		}
   273→	}
   274→}

   277→func (s *FeatureExtractorService) processMessage(msg *nats.Msg) error {

   284→		trace.WithAttributes(attribute.String("event.id", se.ID)))
   285→	defer span.End()
   286→
   287→	// NLP feature extraction
   288→	fv := s.extractFeatures(&se)
   289→
   290→	// Persist to Feature Store
   291→	if err := s.persistFeatureVector(ctx, fv); err != nil {
   292→		span.RecordError(err)

   294→	}
   295→
   296→	// Optional cache
   297→	if s.redis != nil {
   298→		key := fmt.Sprintf("fv:%s", fv.EventID)
   299→		b, _ := json.Marshal(fv)
   300→		if err := s.redis.Set(ctx, key, b, 30*time.Minute).Err(); err != nil {
   301→			log.Printf("[FeatureExtractor] redis set error: %v", err)
   302→		}
   303→	}
   304→
   305→	// Publish downstream
   306→	out, _ := json.Marshal(fv)
   307→	if err := s.js.Publish(ctx, s.cfg.FeatureVectorSubject, out); err != nil {
   308→		span.RecordError(err)
   309→		return fmt.Errorf("publish: %w", err)
   310→	}
   311→
   312→	return nil
   313→}

   315→// extractFeatures runs simple text analytics.  In production this could call
   316→// a more sophisticated pipeline (spaCy server, transformer model, etc.).
   317→func (s *FeatureExtractorService) extractFeatures(se *SocialEvent) *FeatureVector {
   318→	payload := se.Payload
   319→	// Basic heuristics
   320→	wordCount := len(strings.Fields(payload))
   321→	emojiCount := countEmojis(payload)
   322→	var emojiDensity float64
   323→	if wordCount > 0 {
   324→		emojiDensity = float64(emojiCount) / float64(wordCount)
   325→	}
   326→
   327→	// Sentiment
   328→	sentiment := s.analyzer.PolarityScores(payload)
   329→	compound := sentiment.Compound // -1.0 .. 1.0
   330→
   331→	return &FeatureVector{
   332→		EventID:      se.ID,
   333→		UserID:       se.UserID,
   334→		ChannelID:    se.ChannelID,
   335→		Sentiment:    compound,
   336→		EmojiDensity: emojiDensity,
   337→		WordCount:    wordCount,
   338→		Language:     se.Metadata["lang"],
   339→		GeneratedAt:  time.Now().UTC(),
   340→		AdditionalMeta: map[string]string{
   341→			"extractor_version": "v1.3.2",
   342→			"correlation_id":    uuid.NewString(),
   343→		},
   344→	}
   345→}
   346→
   347→// persistFeatureVector upserts the vector in the Feature Store via gRPC.
   348→func (s *FeatureExtractorService) persistFeatureVector(ctx context.Context, fv *FeatureVector) error {
   349→	req := &pb.PutFeatureVectorRequest{
   350→		Vector: &pb.FeatureVector{
   351→			EventId:      fv.EventID,
   352→			UserId:       fv.UserID,
   353→			ChannelId:    fv.ChannelID,
   354→			Sentiment:    fv.Sentiment,
   355→			EmojiDensity: fv.EmojiDensity,
   356→			WordCount:    int32(fv.WordCount),
   357→			Language:     fv.Language,
   358→			GeneratedAt:  fv.GeneratedAt.UnixNano(),
   359→			Metadata:     fv.AdditionalMeta,
   360→		},
   361→	}
   362→	_, err := s.fsClient.PutFeatureVector(ctx, req)
   363→	return err
   364→}
   365→
   366→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   367→// Utility
   368→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   369→
   370→// countEmojis is a naive emoji detection helper.
   371→func countEmojis(s string) int {
   372→	count := 0
   373→	for _, r := range s {
   374→		// Rough range for emoticons + unicode supplemental symbols.
   375→		switch {
   376→		case r >= 0x1F600 && r <= 0x1F64F,
   377→			r >= 0x1F300 && r <= 0x1F5FF,
   378→			r >= 0x1F680 && r <= 0x1F6FF,
   379→			r >= 0x2600 && r <= 0x26FF:
   380→			count++
   381→		}
   382→	}
   383→	return count
   384→}

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"bufio"
     5→	"context"
     6→	"encoding/json"
     7→	"errors"
     8→	"fmt"
     9→	"log"
    10→	"math"
    11→	"math/rand"
    12→	"net/http"
    13→	"os"
    14→	"os/signal"
    15→	"strings"
    16→	"sync"
    17→	"syscall"
    18→	"time"
    19→
    20→	"github.com/segmentio/kafka-go"
    21→
    22→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
    23→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp"
    24→)
    25→
    26→// --------------------------------------------------------
    27→// Domain Models
    28→// --------------------------------------------------------
    29→
    30→// SocialEvent represents the canonical event flowing through the EchoPulse
    31→// backbone.  Down-stream services interpret or augment the event.
    32→type SocialEvent struct {
    33→	ID        string    `json:"id"`
    34→	UserID    string    `json:"user_id"`
    35→	Channel   string    `json:"channel"`
    36→	Timestamp time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
    37→
    38→	Text     string `json:"text,omitempty"`
    39→	Emoji    string `json:"emoji,omitempty"`
    40→	Reaction string `json:"reaction,omitempty"`
    41→}
    42→
    43→// HealthScoreEvent is emitted by this module after computing a real-time
    44→// community health score for a single SocialEvent.
    45→type HealthScoreEvent struct {
    46→	SocialEventID  string             `json:"social_event_id"`
    47→	Score          float64            `json:"score"`
    48→	StrategyScores map[string]float64 `json:"strategy_scores"`
    49→	GeneratedAt    time.Time          `json:"generated_at"`
    50→}
    51→
    52→// --------------------------------------------------------
    53→// Config
    54→// --------------------------------------------------------
    55→
    56→type Config struct {
    57→	KafkaBrokers      []string
    58→	InputTopic        string
    59→	OutputTopic       string
    60→	GroupID           string
    61→	MetricsHTTPAddr   string
    62→	WorkerConcurrency int
    63→	BatchSize         int
    64→	CommitInterval    time.Duration
    65→}
    66→
    67→func loadConfig() Config {
    68→	// In a real system we’d also support flags, config files, Vault, etc.
    69→	brokers := strings.Split(getenvDefault("EP_KAFKA_BROKERS", "localhost:9092"), ",")
    70→	return Config{
    71→		KafkaBrokers:      brokers,
    72→		InputTopic:        getenvDefault("EP_INPUT_TOPIC", "social_events"),
    73→		OutputTopic:       getenvDefault("EP_OUTPUT_TOPIC", "health_scores"),
    74→		GroupID:           getenvDefault("EP_GROUP_ID", "health_scoring_service"),
    75→		MetricsHTTPAddr:   getenvDefault("EP_METRICS_ADDR", ":9090"),
    76→		WorkerConcurrency: getenvIntDefault("EP_WORKERS", 8),
    77→		BatchSize:         getenvIntDefault("EP_BATCH_SIZE", 1),
    78→		CommitInterval:    getenvDurationDefault("EP_COMMIT_INTERVAL", 3*time.Second),
    79→	}
    80→}
    81→
    82→// --------------------------------------------------------
    83→// Strategy Registry
    84→// --------------------------------------------------------
    85→
    86→// ScoringStrategy is pluggable logic that converts a SocialEvent into a
    87→// numerical score.  Higher scores imply healthier community interaction.
    88→type ScoringStrategy interface {
    89→	Name() string
    90→	Score(ctx context.Context, e SocialEvent) (float64, error)
    91→}
    92→
    93→var (
    94→	strategyRegistry = make(map[string]ScoringStrategy)
    95→	registryMu       sync.RWMutex
    96→)
    97→
    98→func RegisterStrategy(s ScoringStrategy) {
    99→	registryMu.Lock()
   100→	defer registryMu.Unlock()
   101→	if _, exists := strategyRegistry[s.Name()]; exists {
   102→		log.Fatalf("strategy %q already registered", s.Name())
   103→	}
   104→	strategyRegistry[s.Name()] = s
   105→}
   106→
   107→func GetStrategies() []ScoringStrategy {
   108→	registryMu.RLock()
   109→	defer registryMu.RUnlock()
   110→	strats := make([]ScoringStrategy, 0, len(strategyRegistry))
   111→	for _, s := range strategyRegistry {
   112→		strats = append(strats, s)
   113→	}
   114→	return strats
   115→}
   116→
   117→// --------------------------------------------------------
   118→// Dummy Strategies (replace with ML models in production)
   119→// --------------------------------------------------------
   120→
   121→type SentimentStrategy struct{}
   122→
   123→func (SentimentStrategy) Name() string { return "sentiment" }
   124→func (SentimentStrategy) Score(_ context.Context, e SocialEvent) (float64, error) {
   125→	if e.Text == "" {
   126→		return 0.5, nil // neutral default
   127→	}
   128→	switch {
   129→	case strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(e.Text), "love"):
   130→		return 0.9, nil
   131→	case strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(e.Text), "hate"):
   132→		return 0.1, nil
   133→	default:
   134→		return 0.5 + rand.Float64()*0.1 - 0.05, nil
   135→	}
   136→}
   137→
   138→type ToxicityStrategy struct{}
   139→
   140→func (ToxicityStrategy) Name() string { return "toxicity" }
   141→func (ToxicityStrategy) Score(_ context.Context, e SocialEvent) (float64, error) {
   142→	// Lower score => less toxic
   143→	if e.Text == "" {
   144→		return 0.9, nil
   145→	}
   146→	switch {
   147→	case strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(e.Text), "idiot"):
   148→		return 0.2, nil
   149→	case strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(e.Text), "kill"):
   150→		return 0.1, nil
   151→	default:
   152→		return 0.8 + rand.Float64()*0.2 - 0.1, nil
   153→	}
   154→}
   155→
   156→// --------------------------------------------------------
   157→// Prometheus Metrics
   158→// --------------------------------------------------------
   159→
   160→var (
   161→	processedEvents = prometheus.NewCounter(
   162→		prometheus.CounterOpts{
   163→			Name: "echopulse_health_processed_events_total",
   164→			Help: "Total number of SocialEvents processed by the health scoring service.",
   165→		},
   166→	)
   167→
   168→	strategyLatency = prometheus.NewHistogramVec(
   169→		prometheus.HistogramOpts{
   170→			Name:    "echopulse_health_strategy_latency_seconds",
   171→			Help:    "Latency distribution for each scoring strategy.",
   172→			Buckets: prometheus.DefBuckets,
   173→		},
   174→		[]string{"strategy"},
   175→	)
   176→
   177→	aggregateLatency = prometheus.NewHistogram(
   178→		prometheus.HistogramOpts{
   179→			Name:    "echopulse_health_aggregate_latency_seconds",
   180→			Help:    "Latency distribution for computing the aggregated health score.",
   181→			Buckets: prometheus.DefBuckets,
   182→		},
   183→	)
   184→)
   185→
   186→func init() {
   187→	// Register builtin strategies
   188→	RegisterStrategy(SentimentStrategy{})
   189→	RegisterStrategy(ToxicityStrategy{})
   190→
   191→	// Register metrics
   192→	prometheus.MustRegister(processedEvents, strategyLatency, aggregateLatency)
   193→}
   194→
   195→// --------------------------------------------------------
   196→// Aggregator
   197→// --------------------------------------------------------
   198→
   199→type Aggregator struct {
   200→	cfg        Config
   201→	strategies []ScoringStrategy
   202→
   203→	reader *kafka.Reader
   204→	writer *kafka.Writer
   205→}
   206→
   207→// NewAggregator composes all required sub-systems for the health scoring loop.
   208→func NewAggregator(cfg Config) *Aggregator {
   209→	reader := kafka.NewReader(kafka.ReaderConfig{
   210→		Brokers:        cfg.KafkaBrokers,
   211→		GroupID:        cfg.GroupID,
   212→		Topic:          cfg.InputTopic,
   213→		MinBytes:       1e4, // 10KB
   214→		MaxBytes:       10e6,
   215→		CommitInterval: cfg.CommitInterval, // explicit commits below
   216→	})
   217→	writer := &kafka.Writer{
   218→		Addr:         kafka.TCP(cfg.KafkaBrokers...),
   219→		Topic:        cfg.OutputTopic,
   220→		RequiredAcks: kafka.RequireAll,
   221→		Balancer:     &kafka.Hash{},
   222→	}
   223→
   224→	return &Aggregator{
   225→		cfg:        cfg,
   226→		strategies: GetStrategies(),
   227→		reader:     reader,
   228→		writer:     writer,
   229→	}
   230→}
   231→
   232→func (a *Aggregator) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
   233→	log.Printf("[aggregator] launching with %d worker(s)", a.cfg.WorkerConcurrency)
   234→	wg := &sync.WaitGroup{}
   235→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
   236→
   237→	// Fan-out workers
   238→	for i := 0; i < a.cfg.WorkerConcurrency; i++ {
   239→		wg.Add(1)
   240→		go func(id int) {
   241→			defer wg.Done()
   242→			a.workerLoop(ctx, id)
   243→		}(i)
   244→	}
   245→
   246→	// Block until context cancelled
   247→	<-ctx.Done()
   248→	log.Println("[aggregator] shutting down workers…")
   249→	cancel()
   250→	wg.Wait()
   251→
   252→	if err := a.reader.Close(); err != nil {
   253→		return fmt.Errorf("close reader: %w", err)
   254→	}
   255→	if err := a.writer.Close(); err != nil {
   256→		return fmt.Errorf("close writer: %w", err)
   257→	}
   258→	return nil
   259→}
   260→
   261→func (a *Aggregator) workerLoop(ctx context.Context, workerID int) {
   262→	for {
   263→		select {
   264→		case <-ctx.Done():
   265→			return
   266→		default:
   267→		}
   268→
   269→		msg, err := a.reader.FetchMessage(ctx)
   270→		if err != nil {
   271→			if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   272→				return
   273→			}
   274→			log.Printf("[worker %d] fetch error: %v", workerID, err)
   275→			continue
   276→		}
   277→
   278→		startAggregate := time.Now()
   279→		var se SocialEvent
   280→		if err := json.Unmarshal(msg.Value, &se); err != nil {
   281→			log.Printf("[worker %d] unmarshal error: %v", workerID, err)
   282→			_ = a.reader.CommitMessages(ctx, msg) // swallow error
   283→			continue
   284→		}
   285→
   286→		healthEvent, err := a.scoreEvent(ctx, se)
   287→		if err != nil {
   288→			log.Printf("[worker %d] scoring error: %v", workerID, err)
   289→			_ = a.reader.CommitMessages(ctx, msg) // swallow error
   290→			continue
   291→		}
   292→
   293→		aggregateLatency.Observe(time.Since(startAggregate).Seconds())
   294→
   295→		if err := a.publishHealth(ctx, healthEvent); err != nil {
   296→			log.Printf("[worker %d] publish error: %v", workerID, err)
   297→			// do not commit so we can retry
   298→			continue
   299→		}
   300→
   301→		if err := a.reader.CommitMessages(ctx, msg); err != nil {
   302→			log.Printf("[worker %d] commit error: %v", workerID, err)
   303→		}
   304→		processedEvents.Inc()
   305→	}
   306→}
   307→
   308→func (a *Aggregator) scoreEvent(ctx context.Context, se SocialEvent) (HealthScoreEvent, error) {
   309→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
   310→	scoreMap := sync.Map{}
   311→	errChan := make(chan error, len(a.strategies))
   312→
   313→	for _, strat := range a.strategies {
   314→		wg.Add(1)
   315→		go func(s ScoringStrategy) {
   316→			defer wg.Done()
   317→			start := time.Now()
   318→			score, err := s.Score(ctx, se)
   319→			if err != nil {
   320→				errChan <- fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", s.Name(), err)
   321→				return
   322→			}
   323→			strategyLatency.WithLabelValues(s.Name()).Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   324→			scoreMap.Store(s.Name(), score)
   325→		}(strat)
   326→	}
   327→
   328→	wg.Wait()
   329→	close(errChan)
   330→	for err := range errChan {
   331→		return HealthScoreEvent{}, err
   332→	}
   333→
   334→	combined := 0.0
   335→	scoreMap.Range(func(key, value interface{}) bool {
   336→		combined += value.(float64)
   337→		return true
   338→	})
   339→	combined = combined / float64(len(a.strategies)) // mean
   340→
   341→	// clamp between 0 and 1
   342→	combined = math.Max(0, math.Min(1, combined))
   343→
   344→	m := make(map[string]float64)
   345→	scoreMap.Range(func(k, v interface{}) bool {
   346→		m[k.(string)] = v.(float64)
   347→		return true
   348→	})
   349→
   350→	return HealthScoreEvent{
   351→		SocialEventID:  se.ID,
   352→		Score:          combined,
   353→		StrategyScores: m,
   354→		GeneratedAt:    time.Now(),
   355→	}, nil
   356→}
   357→
   358→func (a *Aggregator) publishHealth(ctx context.Context, h HealthScoreEvent) error {
   359→	b, err := json.Marshal(h)
   360→	if err != nil {
   361→		return fmt.Errorf("marshal health event: %w", err)
   362→	}
   363→	msg := kafka.Message{
   364→		Key:   []byte(h.SocialEventID),
   365→		Value: b,
   366→		Time:  time.Now(),
   367→	}
   368→	return a.writer.WriteMessages(ctx, msg)
   369→}
   370→
   371→// --------------------------------------------------------
   372→// Main
   373→// --------------------------------------------------------
   374→
   375→func main() {
   376→	cfg := loadConfig()
   377→
   378→	// Spawn metrics server
   379→	go func() {
   380→		http.Handle("/metrics", promhttp.Handler())
   381→		log.Printf("[metrics] listening on %s", cfg.MetricsHTTPAddr)
   382→		if err := http.ListenAndServe(cfg.MetricsHTTPAddr, nil); err != nil {
   383→			log.Fatalf("[metrics] %v", err)
   384→		}
   385→	}()
   386→
   387→	aggregator := NewAggregator(cfg)
   388→
   389→	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
   390→	defer stop()
   391→
   392→	if err := aggregator.Start(ctx); err != nil {
   393→		log.Fatalf("[main] aggregator stopped with error: %v", err)
   394→	}
   395→
   396→	log.Println("[main] graceful shutdown complete")
   397→}
   398→
   399→// --------------------------------------------------------
   400→// Helpers
   401→// --------------------------------------------------------
   402→
   403→func getenvDefault(key, def string) string {
   404→	if v := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(key)); v != "" {
   405→		return v
   406→	}
   407→	return def
   408→}
   409→
   410→func getenvIntDefault(key string, def int) int {
   411→	if v := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(key)); v != "" {
   412→		if i, err := parseInt(v); err == nil {
   413→			return i
   414→		}
   415→	}
   416→	return def
   417→}
   418→
   419→func getenvDurationDefault(key string, def time.Duration) time.Duration {
   420→	if v := strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(key)); v != "" {
   421→		if d, err := time.ParseDuration(v); err == nil {
   422→			return d
   423→		}
   424→	}
   425→	return def
   426→}
   427→
   428→func parseInt(s string) (int, error) {
   429→	r := bufio.NewReader(strings.NewReader(s))
   430→	var x int
   431→	_, err := fmt.Fscan(r, &x)
   432→	return x, err
   433→}

     2→// File: src/module_74.go
     3→//
     4→// EchoPulse :: Real-Time Social Signal Processing Platform
     5→//
     6→// Module 74 – Toxicity Classification Micro-Pipeline
     7→//
     8→// The code in this file wires together a Kafka-backed event processor that
     9→// consumes canonical SocialEvents, applies one (or many) classification
    10→// strategies, and publishes enriched ClassificationEvents back to the event
    11→// bus.  It showcases Observer / Strategy / Pipeline patterns while following
    12→// Go production best-practices: context propagation, graceful shutdown,
    13→// concurrency control, structured logging, metrics, and error handling.
    14→
    15→package processor
    16→
    17→import (
    18→	"bufio"
    19→	"context"
    20→	"encoding/json"
    21→	"errors"
    22→	"math/rand"
    23→	"net"
    24→	"os"
    25→	"os/signal"
    26→	"strings"
    27→	"sync"
    28→	"syscall"
    29→	"time"
    30→
    31→	"github.com/segmentio/kafka-go"
    32→	"go.uber.org/zap"
    33→)
    34→
    35→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36→// Canonical domain types
    37→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→
    39→// SocialEvent is the canonical ingest artefact used across EchoPulse.
    40→type SocialEvent struct {
    41→	ID        string            `json:"id"`
    42→	UserID    string            `json:"user_id"`
    43→	ChannelID string            `json:"channel_id"`
    44→	Payload   string            `json:"payload"` // unified text representation
    45→	Metadata  map[string]string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
    46→	Timestamp time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
    47→}
    48→
    49→// ClassificationEvent is produced by analytic components downstream.
    50→type ClassificationEvent struct {
    51→	SocialEventID string            `json:"social_event_id"`
    52→	Class         string            `json:"class"`
    53→	Score         float64           `json:"score"` // confidence ∈ [0,1]
    54→	Strategy      string            `json:"strategy"`
    55→	Metadata      map[string]string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
    56→	Timestamp     time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
    57→}
    58→
    59→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→// Strategy pattern
    61→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→
    63→// ClassifierStrategy defines the behavioural contract for any classifier.
    64→type ClassifierStrategy interface {
    65→	// Name returns a short identifier (e.g. "toxicity_v1")
    66→	Name() string
    67→	// Classify blocks until the SocialEvent is classified or fails.
    68→	Classify(ctx context.Context, ev SocialEvent) (ClassificationEvent, error)
    69→}
    70→
    71→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→// Toxicity classifier (naïve keyword + model ensemble stub)
    73→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→
    75→// ToxicityStrategy is a demonstration classifier.
    76→// In production this would wrap a model served over gRPC / REST.
    77→type ToxicityStrategy struct {
    78→	// trigram dictionary loaded at start-up
    79→	toxicLexicon map[string]struct{}
    80→	log          *zap.Logger
    81→}
    82→
    83→func NewToxicityStrategy(dictPath string, log *zap.Logger) (*ToxicityStrategy, error) {
    84→	file, err := os.Open(dictPath)
    85→	if err != nil {
    86→		return nil, err
    87→	}
    88→	defer file.Close()
    89→
    90→	lexicon := make(map[string]struct{})
    91→	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(file)
    92→	for scanner.Scan() {
    93→		w := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
    94→		if w != "" {
    95→			lexicon[w] = struct{}{}
    96→		}
    97→	}
    98→	if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
    99→		return nil, err
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	return &ToxicityStrategy{
   103→		toxicLexicon: lexicon,
   104→		log:          log.Named("toxicity_strategy"),
   105→	}, nil
   106→}
   107→
   108→func (s *ToxicityStrategy) Name() string { return "toxicity_v1" }
   109→
   110→func (s *ToxicityStrategy) Classify(ctx context.Context, ev SocialEvent) (ClassificationEvent, error) {
   111→	// A very naïve heuristic: count toxic words to compute score
   112→	words := strings.Fields(strings.ToLower(ev.Payload))
   113→	var toxicCount int
   114→	for _, w := range words {
   115→		if _, ok := s.toxicLexicon[w]; ok {
   116→			toxicCount++
   117→		}
   118→	}
   119→
   120→	// Simulate ensemble model jitter
   121→	rand.Seed(time.Now().UnixNano())
   122→	noise := rand.Float64() * 0.1
   123→
   124→	score := float64(toxicCount)/float64(len(words)+1) + noise // normalize
   125→	if score > 1 {
   126→		score = 1
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	classification := "non_toxic"
   130→	if score >= 0.5 {
   131→		classification = "toxic"
   132→	}
   133→
   134→	return ClassificationEvent{
   135→		SocialEventID: ev.ID,
   136→		Class:         classification,
   137→		Score:         score,
   138→		Strategy:      s.Name(),
   139→		Timestamp:     time.Now().UTC(),
   140→	}, nil
   141→}
   142→
   143→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   144→// Pipeline Orchestrator
   145→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   146→
   147→// ProcessorConfig encapsulates wiring parameters for Processor.
   148→type ProcessorConfig struct {
   149→	Brokers        []string
   150→	GroupID        string
   151→	SourceTopic    string
   152→	SinkTopic      string
   153→	Concurrency    int
   154→	MaxBatchBytes  int
   155→	Strategy       ClassifierStrategy
   156→	Log            *zap.Logger
   157→	CommitInterval time.Duration
   158→}
   159→
   160→// Processor consumes SocialEvents and publishes ClassificationEvents.
   161→type Processor struct {
   162→	cfg     ProcessorConfig
   163→	reader  *kafka.Reader
   164→	writer  *kafka.Writer
   165→	log     *zap.Logger
   166→	wg      sync.WaitGroup
   167→	ctx     context.Context
   168→	cancel  context.CancelFunc
   169→	started bool
   170→}
   171→
   172→// NewProcessor is the factory constructor.
   173→func NewProcessor(cfg ProcessorConfig) (*Processor, error) {
   174→	if cfg.Strategy == nil {
   175→		return nil, errors.New("processor: strategy must not be nil")
   176→	}
   177→	if cfg.Concurrency <= 0 {
   178→		cfg.Concurrency = 4
   179→	}
   180→	if cfg.Log == nil {
   181→		cfg.Log = zap.NewNop()
   182→	}
   183→
   184→	reader := kafka.NewReader(kafka.ReaderConfig{
   185→		Brokers:        cfg.Brokers,
   186→		GroupID:        cfg.GroupID,
   187→		Topic:          cfg.SourceTopic,
   188→		MinBytes:       1e3,  // 1KB
   189→		MaxBytes:       10e6, // 10MB
   190→		MaxWait:        250 * time.Millisecond,
   191→		CommitInterval: cfg.CommitInterval,
   192→	})
   193→	reader.SetOffset(kafka.LastOffset) // catch-up from tail
   194→
   195→	writer := &kafka.Writer{
   196→		Addr:         kafka.TCP(cfg.Brokers...),
   197→		Topic:        cfg.SinkTopic,
   198→		Balancer:     &kafka.Hash{},
   199→		RequiredAcks: kafka.RequireOne,
   200→		Async:        true,
   201→	}
   202→
   203→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
   204→
   205→	return &Processor{
   206→		cfg:    cfg,
   207→		reader: reader,
   208→		writer: writer,
   209→		log:    cfg.Log.Named("processor"),
   210→		ctx:    ctx,
   211→		cancel: cancel,
   212→	}, nil
   213→}
   214→
   215→// Start launches the worker pool and begins consuming messages.
   216→func (p *Processor) Start() {
   217→	if p.started {
   218→		return
   219→	}
   220→	p.started = true
   221→
   222→	p.log.Info("processor starting",
   223→		zap.String("source_topic", p.cfg.SourceTopic),
   224→		zap.String("sink_topic", p.cfg.SinkTopic),
   225→		zap.Int("concurrency", p.cfg.Concurrency),
   226→		zap.String("strategy", p.cfg.Strategy.Name()),
   227→	)
   228→
   229→	// fan-out worker pool
   230→	for i := 0; i < p.cfg.Concurrency; i++ {
   231→		p.wg.Add(1)
   232→		go p.worker(i)
   233→	}
   234→}
   235→
   236→// Stop flushes and terminates all goroutines gracefully.
   237→func (p *Processor) Stop(ctx context.Context) error {
   238→	if !p.started {
   239→		return nil
   240→	}
   241→	p.cancel()
   242→	done := make(chan struct{})
   243→
   244→	go func() {
   245→		defer close(done)
   246→		p.wg.Wait()
   247→	}()
   248→
   249→	select {
   250→	case <-ctx.Done():
   251→		return ctx.Err()
   252→	case <-done:
   253→		// continue
   254→	}
   255→
   256→	if err := p.writer.Close(); err != nil {
   257→		return err
   258→	}
   259→	if err := p.reader.Close(); err != nil {
   260→		return err
   261→	}
   262→
   263→	p.log.Info("processor stopped")
   264→	return nil
   265→}
   266→
   267→// worker runs in a tight loop consuming -> classify -> produce.
   268→func (p *Processor) worker(id int) {
   269→	defer p.wg.Done()
   270→	log := p.log.With(zap.Int("worker_id", id))
   271→	log.Info("worker up")
   272→
   273→	for {
   274→		select {
   275→		case <-p.ctx.Done():
   276→			log.Info("worker shutting down")
   277→			return
   278→		default:
   279→		}
   280→
   281→		m, err := p.reader.FetchMessage(p.ctx)
   282→		if err != nil {
   283→			// The Reader returns ctx.Err upon cancellation—noisy but expected
   284→			if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   285→				return
   286→			}
   287→			log.Warn("fetch message error", zap.Error(err))
   288→			continue
   289→		}
   290→
   291→		var se SocialEvent
   292→		if err := json.Unmarshal(m.Value, &se); err != nil {
   293→			log.Error("unmarshal social event", zap.Error(err))
   294→			_ = p.reader.CommitMessages(p.ctx, m) // commit poison pill to avoid retry loop
   295→			continue
   296→		}
   297→
   298→		ce, err := p.cfg.Strategy.Classify(p.ctx, se)
   299→		if err != nil {
   300→			log.Error("classify error", zap.Error(err))
   301→			continue
   302→		}
   303→
   304→		payload, _ := json.Marshal(ce)
   305→
   306→		msg := kafka.Message{
   307→			Key:   []byte(ce.SocialEventID),
   308→			Value: payload,
   309→			Time:  time.Now(),
   310→		}
   311→
   312→		// Asynchronous writer; handle error in completion.
   313→		if err := p.writer.WriteMessages(p.ctx, msg); err != nil {
   314→			log.Error("write message error", zap.Error(err))
   315→			// Optionally retry or store in DLQ
   316→		}
   317→
   318→		if err := p.reader.CommitMessages(p.ctx, m); err != nil {
   319→			log.Warn("commit offset error", zap.Error(err))
   320→		}
   321→	}
   322→}

   324→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   325→// Bootstrap (optional) :: run as standalone binary
   326→// -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   327→
   328→// Main is separated to allow the file to compile as part of a larger repo
   329→// without forcing inclusion in cmd/... packages.  The build tag ensures it
   330→// is only included when desired.
   331→//
   332→//	//go:build module74main
   333→//
   334→// To run locally:
   335→// $ go run -tags=module74main ./src/module_74.go -brokers localhost:9092
   336→//
   337→
   338→//go:build module74main
   339→// +build module74main
   340→
   341→package main
   342→
   343→import (
   344→	"flag"
   345→	"time"
   346→
   347→	"go.uber.org/zap"
   348→)
   349→
   350→func main() {
   351→	var (
   352→		broker       = flag.String("brokers", "localhost:9092", "comma-delimited list of brokers")
   353→		group        = flag.String("group", "echopulse_toxicity", "consumer group id")
   354→		sourceTopic  = flag.String("source", "social_events", "source topic")
   355→		sinkTopic    = flag.String("sink", "classification_events", "sink topic")
   356→		dictPath     = flag.String("dict", "./toxicity_lexicon.txt", "path to toxicity dictionary")
   357→		concurrency  = flag.Int("concurrency", 8, "number of workers")
   358→		commitMillis = flag.Int("commit_interval_ms", 500, "offset commit interval (ms)")
   359→	)
   360→	flag.Parse()
   361→
   362→	log, _ := zap.NewProduction()
   363→	defer log.Sync()
   364→
   365→	strategy, err := NewToxicityStrategy(*dictPath, log)
   366→	if err != nil {
   367→		log.Fatal("failed to init strategy", zap.Error(err))
   368→	}
   369→
   370→	cfg := ProcessorConfig{
   371→		Brokers:        strings.Split(*broker, ","),
   372→		GroupID:        *group,
   373→		SourceTopic:    *sourceTopic,
   374→		SinkTopic:      *sinkTopic,
   375→		Concurrency:    *concurrency,
   376→		MaxBatchBytes:  10e6,
   377→		Strategy:       strategy,
   378→		Log:            log,
   379→		CommitInterval: time.Duration(*commitMillis) * time.Millisecond,
   380→	}
   381→
   382→	processor, err := NewProcessor(cfg)
   383→	if err != nil {
   384→		log.Fatal("failed to create processor", zap.Error(err))
   385→	}
   386→
   387→	processor.Start()
   388→
   389→	// Wait for SIGINT/SIGTERM
   390→	sig := make(chan os.Signal, 2)
   391→	signal.Notify(sig, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   392→	<-sig
   393→	log.Info("shutdown signal received")
   394→
   395→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
   396→	defer cancel()
   397→
   398→	if err := processor.Stop(ctx); err != nil {
   399→		log.Error("graceful stop failed", zap.Error(err))
   400→	}
   401→}
   402→
   403→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   404→// Utility helpers
   405→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   406→
   407→// isPortOpen checks if the network endpoint is reachable (used by health probes).
   408→func isPortOpen(addr string, timeout time.Duration) bool {
   409→	conn, err := net.DialTimeout("tcp", addr, timeout)
   410→	if err != nil {
   411→		return false
   412→	}
   413→	_ = conn.Close()
   414→	return true
   415→}

-------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Strategy pattern\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// ClassifierStrategy Toxicity classifier (naïve keyword + model ensemble stub)\n// -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// ToxicityStrategy is a demonstration classifier.\n// In production this would wrap a model served over gRPC / REST.\ntype ToxicityStrategy struct {\n\t// trigram dictionary loaded at start-up\n\ttoxicLexicon map[string]struct{}\n\tlog          *zap.Logger\n}\n\nfunc NewToxicityStrategy(dictPath string, log *zap.Logger) (*ToxicityStrategy, error) {\n\tfile, err := os.Open(dictPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tdefer file.Close()\n\n\tlexicon := make(map[string]struct{})\n\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(file)\n\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n\t\tw := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\n\t\tif w != \"\" {\n\t\t\tlexicon[w] = struct{}{}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ToxicityStrategy{\n\t\ttoxicLexicon: lexicon,\n\t\tlog:          log.Named(\"toxicity_strategy\"),\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *ToxicityStrategy) Name() string { return \"toxicity_v1\" }\n\nfunc (s *ToxicityStrategy) Classify(ctx context.Context, ev SocialEvent) (ClassificationEvent, error) {\n\t// A very naïve heuristic: count toxic words to compute score\n\twords := strings.Fields(strings.ToLower(ev.Payload))\n\tvar toxicCount int\n\tfor _, w := range words {\n\t\tif _, ok := s.toxicLexicon[w]; ok {\n\t\t\ttoxicCount++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Simulate ensemble model jitter\n\trand.Seed(time.Now().UnixNano())\n\tnoise := rand.Float64() * 0.1\n\n\tscore := float64(toxicCount)/float64(len(words)+1) + noise // normalize\n\tif score > 1 {\n\t\tscore = 1\n\t}\n\n\tclassification := \"non_toxic\"\n\tif score >= 0.5 {\n\t\tclassification = \"toxic\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn ClassificationEvent{\n\t\tSocialEventID: ev.ID,\n\t\tClass:         classification,\n\t\tScore:         score,\n\t\tStrategy:      s.Name(),\n\t\tTimestamp: <= 0 {\n\t\tcfg.Concurrency = 4\n\t}\n\tif cfg.Log == nil {\n\t\tcfg.Log = zap.NewNop()\n\t}\n\n\treader := kafka.NewReader(kafka.ReaderConfig{\n\t\tBrokers:        cfg.Brokers,\n\t\tGroupID:        cfg.GroupID,\n\t\tTopic:          cfg.SourceTopic,\n\t\tMinBytes:       1e3,  // 1KB\n\t\tMaxBytes:       10e6, // 10MB\n\t\tMaxWait:        250 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tCommitInterval: cfg.CommitInterval,\n\t})\n\treader.SetOffset(kafka.LastOffset) // catch-up from tail\n\n\twriter := &kafka.Writer{\n\t\tAddr:         kafka.TCP(cfg.Brokers...),\n\t\tTopic:        cfg.SinkTopic,\n\t\tBalancer:     &kafka.Hash{},\n\t\tRequiredAcks: kafka.RequireOne,\n\t\tAsync:        true,\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\n\treturn pool and begins consuming messages.\nfunc (p *Processor) Start() {\n\tif p.started {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tp.started = true\n\n\tp.log.Info(\"processor starting\",\n\t\tzap.String(\"source_topic\", p.cfg.SourceTopic),\n\t\tzap.String(\"sink_topic\", p.cfg.SinkTopic),\n\t\tzap.Int(\"concurrency\", p.cfg.Concurrency),\n\t\tzap.String(\"strategy\", p.cfg.Strategy.Name()),\n\t)\n\n\t// fan-out worker pool\n\tfor i := 0; i < p.cfg.Concurrency; i++ {\n\t\tp.wg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo p.worker(i)\n\t}\n}\n\n// Stop flushes and terminates all goroutines gracefully.\nfunc (p *Processor) Stop(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tif !p.started {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tp.cancel()\n\tdone := make(chan struct{})\n\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer close(done)\n\t\tp.wg.Wait()\n\t}()\n\n\tselect {\n\tcase err\n\t}\n\n\tp.log.Info(\"processor stopped\")\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// worker runs in a tight loop consuming -> classify -> produce.\nfunc (p *Processor) worker(id int) {\n\tdefer p.wg.Done()\n\tlog := p.log.With(zap.Int(\"worker_id\", id))\n\tlog.Info(\"worker up\")\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-p.ctx.Done():\n\t\t\tlog.Info(\"worker shutting down\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tm, err := p.reader.FetchMessage(p.ctx)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// The Reader returns ctx.Err upon cancellation—noisy but expected\n\t\t\tif json.Unmarshal(m.Value, &se); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Error(\"unmarshal social event\", zap.Error(err))\n\t\t\t_ = p.reader.CommitMessages(p.ctx, m) // commit poison pill to avoid retry loop\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tce, err := p.cfg.Strategy.Classify(p.ctx, se)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Error(\"classify error\", zap.Error(err))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tpayload, _ := json.Marshal(ce)\n\n\t\tmsg := kafka.Message{\n\t\t\tKey:   []byte(ce.SocialEventID),\n\t\t\tValue: payload,\n\t\t\tTime:  time.Now(),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Asynchronous writer; handle error in completion.\n\t\tif err := p.writer.WriteMessages(p.ctx, msg); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Error(\"write message error\", zap.Error(err))\n\t\t\t// Optionally retry or store in DLQ\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif err := p.reader.CommitMessages(p.ctx, m); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Warn(\"commit offset error\", zap.Error(err))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// localhost:9092\n//\n\n//go:build module74main\n// +build module74main\n\npackage main\n\nimport (\n\t\"flag\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"go.uber.org/zap\"\n)\n\nfunc main() {\n\tvar (\n\t\tbroker       =
