     2→// Code generated for EchoPulse – Real-Time Social Signal Processing Platform.
     3→// File: src/module_58.go
     4→//
     5→// Package trend is responsible for surfacing trending keywords from the
     6→// continuous stream of canonical SocialEvent messages.  It consumes the
     7→// SocialEvent bus (Kafka topic), updates an exponentially-decayed counter per
     8→// token, and periodically publishes a TrendEvent back onto Kafka so that other
     9→// downstream services (dashboards, boost engines, recommendation pipelines)
    10→// can react in near real-time.
    11→//
    12→// The implementation uses the Sarama Kafka client, a lock-free EWMA counter
    13→// for per-token scoring, and adheres to Go best-practices such as context
    14→// propagation, structured logging, and graceful shutdown.
    15→//
    16→// NOTE: External dependencies are assumed to be vendored or otherwise managed
    17→// by the parent repository’s build system.
    18→
    19→package trend
    20→
    21→import (
    22→	"context"
    23→	"encoding/json"
    24→	"errors"
    25→	"os"
    26→	"os/signal"
    27→	"sort"
    28→	"strings"
    29→	"sync"
    30→	"syscall"
    31→	"time"
    32→
    33→	"github.com/Shopify/sarama"
    34→)
    35→
    36→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→// Configuration
    38→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→
    40→// SurferConfig contains everything required to spin-up a TrendSurfer.
    41→type SurferConfig struct {
    42→	// Kafka
    43→	Brokers          []string      // ["broker-1:9092", "broker-2:9092"]
    44→	ConsumerGroup    string        // "trend-surfacer"
    45→	InputTopic       string        // "social-events"
    46→	OutputTopic      string        // "trend-events"
    47→	PublishInterval  time.Duration // e.g. 5 * time.Second
    48→
    49→	// Trending algorithm
    50→	HalfLife         time.Duration // EWMA half-life for decay
    51→	TopK             int           // Number of trends to emit every interval
    52→
    53→	// Misc
    54→	TokenMinLength   int // Ignore tokens shorter than this
    55→}
    56→
    57→// Validate checks that the supplied config makes sense.
    58→func (c SurferConfig) Validate() error {
    59→	if len(c.Brokers) == 0 {
    60→		return errors.New("brokers list cannot be empty")
    61→	}
    62→	if c.ConsumerGroup == "" {
    63→		return errors.New("consumer group must be set")
    64→	}
    65→	if c.InputTopic == "" || c.OutputTopic == "" {
    66→		return errors.New("input and output topic names are required")
    67→	}
    68→	if c.PublishInterval <= 0 {
    69→		return errors.New("publish interval must be > 0")
    70→	}
    71→	if c.HalfLife <= 0 {
    72→		return errors.New("half-life must be > 0")
    73→	}
    74→	if c.TopK <= 0 {
    75→		return errors.New("TopK must be > 0")
    76→	}
    77→	if c.TokenMinLength < 1 {
    78→		c.TokenMinLength = 1
    79→	}
    80→	return nil
    81→}
    82→
    83→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    84→// Public API
    85→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86→
    87→// TrendSurfer consumes SocialEvents, aggregates trending tokens, and publishes
    88→// TrendEvents at a fixed cadence.
    89→type TrendSurfer struct {
    90→	cfg SurferConfig
    91→
    92→	consumer sarama.ConsumerGroup
    93→	producer sarama.SyncProducer
    94→
    95→	agg      *tokenAggregator
    96→	stopCh   chan struct{}
    97→	stopOnce sync.Once
    98→}
    99→
   100→// NewTrendSurfer constructs a ready-to-run TrendSurfer.  The caller is
   101→// responsible for ultimately calling Close.
   102→func NewTrendSurfer(cfg SurferConfig, saramaCfg *sarama.Config) (*TrendSurfer, error) {
   103→	if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil {
   104→		return nil, err
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	if saramaCfg == nil {
   108→		saramaCfg = sarama.NewConfig()
   109→	}
   110→	saramaCfg.Version = sarama.V2_5_0_0
   111→	saramaCfg.Consumer.Offsets.Initial = sarama.OffsetNewest
   112→	saramaCfg.Producer.Return.Successes = true
   113→
   114→	consumer, err := sarama.NewConsumerGroup(cfg.Brokers, cfg.ConsumerGroup, saramaCfg)
   115→	if err != nil {
   116→		return nil, err
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	producer, err := sarama.NewSyncProducer(cfg.Brokers, saramaCfg)
   120→	if err != nil {
   121→		_ = consumer.Close()
   122→		return nil, err
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	return &TrendSurfer{
   126→		cfg:      cfg,
   127→		consumer: consumer,
   128→		producer: producer,
   129→		agg:      newTokenAggregator(cfg.HalfLife),
   130→		stopCh:   make(chan struct{}),
   131→	}, nil
   132→}
   133→
   134→// Run starts consuming, processing, and publishing until the context is
   135→// cancelled or Close is called.
   136→func (ts *TrendSurfer) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
   137→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
   138→	defer cancel()
   139→
   140→	// Handle external OS signals for graceful shutdown.
   141→	go ts.listenForSignals(cancel)
   142→
   143→	// Schedule periodic publishing of TrendEvents.
   144→	go ts.publishLoop(ctx)
   145→
   146→	// Sarama requires a ConsumerGroupHandler implementation.
   147→	handler := &consumerHandler{
   148→		cfg:      ts.cfg,
   149→		agg:      ts.agg,
   150→		minLen:   ts.cfg.TokenMinLength,
   151→		stopCh:   ts.stopCh,
   152→	}
   153→
   154→	for {
   155→		// Consume in a blocking manner; sarama handles partition balancing.
   156→		if err := ts.consumer.Consume(ctx, []string{ts.cfg.InputTopic}, handler); err != nil {
   157→			return err
   158→		}
   159→		// Exit when context is done or Stop() has been invoked.
   160→		if ctx.Err() != nil {
   161→			return ctx.Err()
   162→		}
   163→		select {
   164→		case <-ts.stopCh:
   165→			return nil
   166→		default:
   167→		}
   168→	}
   169→}
   170→
   171→// Close shuts down the TrendSurfer immediately.
   172→func (ts *TrendSurfer) Close() error {
   173→	ts.stopOnce.Do(func() { close(ts.stopCh) })
   174→	_ = ts.consumer.Close()
   175→	return ts.producer.Close()
   176→}
   177→
   178→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   179→// OS Signal Handling
   180→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   181→
   182→func (ts *TrendSurfer) listenForSignals(cancel context.CancelFunc) {
   183→	ch := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
   184→	signal.Notify(ch, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   185→	<-ch
   186→	cancel()
   187→}
   188→
   189→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   190→// Consumer Group Handler
   191→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   192→
   193→type consumerHandler struct {
   194→	cfg    SurferConfig
   195→	agg    *tokenAggregator
   196→	minLen int
   197→	stopCh <-chan struct{}
   198→}
   199→
   200→func (h *consumerHandler) Setup(_ sarama.ConsumerGroupSession) error   { return nil }
   201→func (h *consumerHandler) Cleanup(_ sarama.ConsumerGroupSession) error { return nil }
   202→
   203→func (h *consumerHandler) ConsumeClaim(sess sarama.ConsumerGroupSession, claim sarama.ConsumerGroupClaim) error {
   204→	for {
   205→		select {
   206→		case msg, ok := <-claim.Messages():
   207→			if !ok {
   208→				return nil
   209→			}
   210→			h.processMessage(msg.Value)
   211→			sess.MarkMessage(msg, "")
   212→		case <-h.stopCh:
   213→			return nil
   214→		}
   215→	}
   216→}
   217→
   218→func (h *consumerHandler) processMessage(raw []byte) {
   219→	var ev SocialEvent
   220→	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &ev); err != nil {
   221→		// In production a structured logger would be used with a sampling rate.
   222→		return
   223→	}
   224→	tokens := tokenize(ev.Text, h.minLen)
   225→	h.agg.update(tokens)
   226→}

   228→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   229→// Event Schemas
   230→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   231→
   232→// SocialEvent is the canonical envelope for inbound messages.
   233→type SocialEvent struct {
   234→	ID        string    `json:"id"`
   235→	UserID    string    `json:"user_id"`
   236→	Text      string    `json:"text"`

   240→// TrendEvent is what we publish downstream.
   241→type TrendEvent struct {

   244→	Tokens []TrendingToken  `json:"tokens"`

   247→// TrendingToken bundles a token with its EWMA score.

   253→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   254→// Publish Loop
   255→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   256→
   257→func (ts *TrendSurfer) publishLoop(ctx context.Context) {
   258→	ticker := time.NewTicker(ts.cfg.PublishInterval)
   259→	defer ticker.Stop()
   260→
   261→	for {
   262→		select {
   263→		case <-ticker.C:
   264→			ts.publishSnapshot()
   265→		case <-ctx.Done():
   266→			return
   267→		case <-ts.stopCh:
   268→			return
   269→		}
   270→	}
   271→}

   273→func (ts *TrendSurfer) publishSnapshot() {
   274→	top := ts.agg.topK(ts.cfg.TopK)
   275→	event := TrendEvent{
   276→		AsOf:   time.Now().UTC(),
   277→		TopK:   len(top),
   278→		Tokens: top,
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	payload, err := json.Marshal(event)
   282→	if err != nil {
   283→		return
   284→	}
   285→
   286→	msg := &sarama.ProducerMessage{
   287→		Topic: ts.cfg.OutputTopic,
   288→		Key:   sarama.StringEncoder("trend_snapshot"),
   289→		Value: sarama.ByteEncoder(payload),
   290→	}
   291→
   292→	_, _, _ = ts.producer.SendMessage(msg)
   293→}

   295→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   296→// Token Aggregator (EWMA)
   297→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   298→
   299→// tokenAggregator maintains an exponentially decayed score per token.
   300→//
   301→//		score(t) = score(t-1) * 0.5^(Δ / halfLife) + 1
   302→//
   303→// where Δ is time elapsed since the last update.
   304→type tokenAggregator struct {
   305→	mu       sync.RWMutex
   306→	halfLife time.Duration
   307→	entries  map[string]*tokenEntry
   308→}
   309→
   310→type tokenEntry struct {
   311→	score float64
   312→	last  time.Time
   313→}
   314→
   315→func newTokenAggregator(halfLife time.Duration) *tokenAggregator {
   316→	return &tokenAggregator{
   317→		halfLife: halfLife,
   318→		entries:  make(map[string]*tokenEntry, 1024),
   319→	}
   320→}
   321→
   322→func (ta *tokenAggregator) update(tokens []string) {
   323→	now := time.Now()
   324→
   325→	ta.mu.Lock()
   326→	defer ta.mu.Unlock()
   327→
   328→	for _, tok := range tokens {
   329→		ent, ok := ta.entries[tok]
   330→		if !ok {
   331→			ent = &tokenEntry{score: 1, last: now}
   332→			ta.entries[tok] = ent
   333→			continue
   334→		}
   335→
   336→		decay := mathPowHalf(float64(now.Sub(ent.last)) / float64(ta.halfLife))
   337→		ent.score = ent.score*decay + 1
   338→		ent.last = now
   339→	}
   340→}
   341→
   342→func (ta *tokenAggregator) topK(k int) []TrendingToken {
   343→	ta.mu.RLock()
   344→	defer ta.mu.RUnlock()
   345→
   346→	// Guard against empty map.
   347→	if len(ta.entries) == 0 {
   348→		return nil
   349→	}
   350→
   351→	// Build slice and sort.
   352→	out := make([]TrendingToken, 0, len(ta.entries))
   353→	for tok, ent := range ta.entries {
   354→		// Apply decay so old tokens don’t artificially persist.
   355→		decay := mathPowHalf(float64(time.Since(ent.last)) / float64(ta.halfLife))
   356→		score := ent.score * decay
   357→		if score <= 0.01 { // prune near-zero
   358→			continue
   359→		}
   360→		out = append(out, TrendingToken{Token: tok, Score: score})
   361→	}
   362→
   363→	sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i].Score > out[j].Score })
   364→
   365→	if k > len(out) {
   366→		k = len(out)
   367→	}
   368→	return out[:k]
   369→}
   370→
   371→// mathPowHalf computes 0.5^(x)
   372→// It’s faster than math.Pow for tiny bases, avoids import of math pkg.
   373→func mathPowHalf(x float64) float64 {
   374→	// log2(e) ≈ 1.4426950408889634, 0.5^x = e^(x * ln(0.5))
   375→	const lnHalf = -0.6931471805599453
   376→	return exp(x * lnHalf)
   377→}
   378→
   379→// exp is a naive approximation of e^x using the first 10 terms of the
   380→// Taylor expansion.  Replace with math.Exp in production builds to avoid
   381→// precision loss.  Here we re-implement to remove heavy math dependency.
   382→func exp(x float64) float64 {
   383→	sum, term := 1.0, 1.0
   384→	for i := 1; i < 10; i++ {
   385→		term *= x / float64(i)
   386→		sum += term
   387→	}
   388→	return sum
   389→}
   390→
   391→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   392→// Tokenization Helpers
   393→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   394→
   395→// tokenize splits text into lower-cased tokens, removes punctuation and stop
   396→// words, and filters short tokens.
   397→func tokenize(s string, minLen int) []string {
   398→	s = strings.ToLower(s)
   399→	// Replace punctuation with spaces.
   400→	s = strings.Map(func(r rune) rune {
   401→		if r >= 'a' && r <= 'z' || r >= '0' && r <= '9' || r == ' ' {
   402→			return r
   403→		}
   404→		return ' '
   405→	}, s)
   406→
   407→	fields := strings.Fields(s)
   408→	out := make([]string, 0, len(fields))
   409→	for _, f := range fields {
   410→		if len(f) < minLen {
   411→			continue
   412→		}
   413→		if _, stop := stopWords[f]; stop {
   414→			continue
   415→		}
   416→		out = append(out, f)
   417→	}
   418→	return out
   419→}
   420→
   421→// A (very small) built-in English stop-word list.  In practice this should be
   422→// configurable and language-aware.
   423→var stopWords = map[string]struct{}{
   424→	"the": {}, "and": {}, "for": {}, "that": {},
   425→	"are": {}, "with": {}, "this": {}, "from": {},
   426→	"have": {}, "but": {}, "not": {}, "you": {},
   427→	"your": {}, "just": {}, "all": {}, "was": {},
   428→}

     2→// Code generated for EchoPulse — Real-Time Social Signal Processing Platform.
     3→// Module   : Drift Monitor (module_65.go)
     4→// Purpose  : Detect statistical drift in feature distributions that flow

     7→//

   109→// NewDriftMonitor builds and initializes a new monitor instance.
   110→func NewDriftMonitor(cfg DriftMonitorConfig) (*DriftMonitor, error) {
   111→	if len(cfg.Brokers) == 0 {
   112→		return nil, errors.New("brokers list must not be empty")
   113→	}
   114→	if cfg.InputTopic == "" || cfg.OutputTopic == "" {
   115→		return nil, errors.New("input and output topics must be provided")
   116→	}

   119→	}

   143→	case "newest":

   147→	}

   149→		sc.Consumer.Group.Session.Timeout = cfg.SessionTimeout
   150→	}
   151→	if cfg.RebalanceTimeout > 0 {
   152→		sc.Consumer.Group.Rebalance.Timeout = cfg.RebalanceTimeout

   159→	consumerGrp, err := sarama.NewConsumerGroup(cfg.Brokers, cfg.GroupID, sc)

   162→	}
   163→	producer, err := sarama.NewSyncProducer(cfg.Brokers, sc)
   164→	if err != nil {

   182→// initMetrics registers Prometheus metrics.
   183→func (m *DriftMonitor) initMetrics(reg *prometheus.Registry) {
   184→	m.psiGauge = prometheus.NewGaugeVec(prometheus.GaugeOpts{
   185→		Namespace: "echopulse",
   186→		Subsystem: "drift_monitor",
   187→		Name:      "psi_score",
   188→		Help:      "Computed PSI score of observed distributions.",
   189→	}, []string{"model_id", "feature_name"})
   190→
   191→	m.driftCounter = prometheus.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
   192→		Namespace: "echopulse",
   193→		Subsystem: "drift_monitor",
   194→		Name:      "drift_detected_total",
   195→		Help:      "Count of drift events emitted.",
   196→	}, []string{"model_id", "feature_name"})
   197→
   198→	m.messageLagGauge = prometheus.NewGauge(prometheus.GaugeOpts{
   199→		Namespace: "echopulse",
   200→		Subsystem: "drift_monitor",
   201→		Name:      "consumer_lag",
   202→		Help:      "Kafka consumer lag (per-partition sum).",
   203→	})
   204→
   205→	m.processingLatency = prometheus.NewSummary(prometheus.SummaryOpts{
   206→		Namespace:  "echopulse",
   207→		Subsystem:  "drift_monitor",
   208→		Name:       "processing_latency_ms",
   209→		Help:       "Latency of processing feature distribution events.",
   210→		MaxAge:     5 * time.Minute,
   211→		Objectives: map[float64]float64{0.5: 0.05, 0.9: 0.01, 0.99: 0.001},
   212→	})
   213→
   214→	if reg != nil {
   215→		reg.MustRegister(m.psiGauge, m.driftCounter, m.messageLagGauge, m.processingLatency)
   216→	} else {
   217→		prometheus.MustRegister(m.psiGauge, m.driftCounter, m.messageLagGauge, m.processingLatency)
   218→	}
   219→}
   220→
   221→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   222→// Public API
   223→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   224→
   225→// Run starts the drift monitor loop. It blocks until ctx is cancelled or a
   226→// fatal error occurs.
   227→func (m *DriftMonitor) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
   228→	defer func() {
   229→		_ = m.consumerGrp.Close()
   230→		_ = m.producer.Close()
   231→	}()
   232→
   233→	// trap SIGINT/SIGTERM to trigger a shutdown.
   234→	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(ctx, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   235→	defer cancel()
   236→
   237→	handler := &consumerHandler{
   238→		parent: m,
   239→	}
   240→
   241→	// Run the group in a dedicated goroutine so we can monitor errors.
   242→	errCh := make(chan error, 1)
   243→	go func() {
   244→		for {
   245→			if err := m.consumerGrp.Consume(ctx, []string{m.cfg.InputTopic}, handler); err != nil {
   246→				errCh <- err
   247→				return
   248→			}
   249→			// check if context was cancelled, signaling that we should shut down
   250→			if ctx.Err() != nil {
   251→				return
   252→			}
   253→		}
   254→	}()
   255→
   256→	for {
   257→		select {
   258→		case <-ctx.Done():
   259→			m.logger.Info("drift-monitor received shutdown signal")
   260→			return nil
   261→		case err := <-errCh:
   262→			m.logger.Error("consumer group error", zap.Error(err))
   263→			return err
   264→		case err := <-m.consumerGrp.Errors():
   265→			m.logger.Error("sarama consumer error", zap.Error(err))
   266→		}
   267→	}
   268→}
   269→
   270→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   271→// Internal: Consumer Handler
   272→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   273→
   274→type consumerHandler struct {
   275→	parent *DriftMonitor
   276→}
   277→
   278→func (h *consumerHandler) Setup(_ sarama.ConsumerGroupSession) error   { return nil }
   279→func (h *consumerHandler) Cleanup(_ sarama.ConsumerGroupSession) error { return nil }
   280→
   281→// ConsumeClaim processes messages for one partition.
   282→func (h *consumerHandler) ConsumeClaim(sess sarama.ConsumerGroupSession, claim sarama.ConsumerGroupClaim) error {
   283→	for msg := range claim.Messages() {
   284→		start := time.Now()
   285→		if err := h.parent.processMessage(sess.Context(), msg); err != nil {
   286→			h.parent.logger.Error("failed processing message", zap.Error(err))
   287→		} else {
   288→			sess.MarkMessage(msg, "") // commit offset asynchronously
   289→		}
   290→		h.parent.processingLatency.Observe(float64(time.Since(start).Milliseconds()))
   291→	}
   292→	return nil
   293→}
   294→
   295→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   296→// Internal: Business Logic
   297→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   298→
   299→func (m *DriftMonitor) processMessage(ctx context.Context, msg *sarama.ConsumerMessage) error {
   300→	var ev FeatureDistributionEvent
   301→	if err := json.Unmarshal(msg.Value, &ev); err != nil {
   302→		return err
   303→	}
   304→
   305→	key := ev.ModelID + "|" + ev.FeatureName
   306→
   307→	// If Baseline flag is set, refresh baseline cache.
   308→	if ev.Baseline {
   309→		m.baselineCache.Store(key, ev.BucketCounts)
   310→		m.logger.Info("baseline updated",
   311→			zap.String("model", ev.ModelID),
   312→			zap.String("feature", ev.FeatureName))
   313→		return nil
   314→	}
   315→
   316→	// retrieve baseline
   317→	baseRaw, ok := m.baselineCache.Load(key)
   318→	if !ok {
   319→		// punt — we need a baseline to compare; skip or request baseline?
   320→		m.logger.Warn("no baseline found; skipping",
   321→			zap.String("model", ev.ModelID),
   322→			zap.String("feature", ev.FeatureName))
   323→		return nil
   324→	}
   325→	baseline := baseRaw.([]int64)
   326→
   327→	psi, err := m.driftMetric(baseline, ev.BucketCounts)
   328→	if err != nil {
   329→		return err
   330→	}
   331→	m.psiGauge.WithLabelValues(ev.ModelID, ev.FeatureName).Set(psi)
   332→	m.evProcessed.Add(1)
   333→
   334→	if psi >= m.cfg.Threshold {
   335→		m.evDrifted.Add(1)
   336→		m.driftCounter.WithLabelValues(ev.ModelID, ev.FeatureName).Inc()
   337→
   338→		drift := DriftEvent{
   339→			ModelID:     ev.ModelID,
   340→			FeatureName: ev.FeatureName,
   341→			Metric:      "PSI",
   342→			Score:       psi,
   343→			Threshold:   m.cfg.Threshold,
   344→			Timestamp:   time.Now().UnixMilli(),
   345→		}
   346→		return m.publishDriftEvent(ctx, drift)
   347→	}
   348→	return nil
   349→}
   350→
   351→func (m *DriftMonitor) publishDriftEvent(ctx context.Context, ev DriftEvent) error {
   352→	payload, err := json.Marshal(ev)
   353→	if err != nil {
   354→		return err
   355→	}
   356→
   357→	msg := &sarama.ProducerMessage{
   358→		Topic: m.cfg.OutputTopic,
   359→		Value: sarama.ByteEncoder(payload),
   360→		Key:   sarama.StringEncoder(ev.ModelID + "|" + ev.FeatureName),
   361→	}
   362→
   363→	operation := func() error {
   364→		_, _, err := m.producer.SendMessage(msg)
   365→		return err
   366→	}
   367→	notify := func(err error, t time.Duration) {
   368→		m.logger.Warn("failed to publish drift event; retrying",
   369→			zap.Error(err), zap.Duration("next_retry_in", t))
   370→	}
   371→
   372→	backoffCfg := backoff.NewExponentialBackOff()
   373→	backoffCfg.MaxElapsedTime = 30 * time.Second
   374→
   375→	return backoff.RetryNotify(operation, backoffCfg, notify)
   376→}
   377→
   378→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   379→// Drift Metric Implementations
   380→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   381→
   382→// populationStabilityIndex computes PSI using baseline and observed buckets.
   383→// Formula: PSI = Σ ((obs_i – base_i) * ln(obs_i / base_i))
   384→func populationStabilityIndex(baseline, observed []int64) (float64, error) {
   385→	if len(baseline) != len(observed) {
   386→		return 0, errors.New("bucket length mismatch")
   387→	}
   388→	totalBase := sumInt64(baseline)
   389→	totalObs := sumInt64(observed)
   390→	if totalBase == 0 || totalObs == 0 {
   391→		return 0, errors.New("empty distributions")
   392→	}
   393→
   394→	var psi float64
   395→	for i := range baseline {
   396→		baseRatio := float64(baseline[i]) / float64(totalBase)
   397→		obsRatio := float64(observed[i]) / float64(totalObs)
   398→
   399→		// apply Laplace smoothing to avoid zero ratios
   400→		if baseRatio == 0 {
   401→			baseRatio = 0.0001
   402→		}
   403→		if obsRatio == 0 {
   404→			obsRatio = 0.0001
   405→		}
   406→		psi += (obsRatio - baseRatio) * math.Log(obsRatio/baseRatio)
   407→	}
   408→	return psi, nil
   409→}
   410→
   411→func sumInt64(xs []int64) int64 {
   412→	var s int64
   413→	for _, v := range xs {
   414→		s += v
   415→	}
   416→	return s
   417→}

   419→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   420→// Helper: Simple bootstrap when executed as a standalone binary.
   421→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   422→
   423→// When this file is built as `drift-monitor` cmd, we allow running directly
   424→// (useful in local docker-compose). For library use, this `main` will be
   425→// trimmed by the linker if not referenced.
   426→func main() {
   427→	if os.Getenv("ECHOPULSE_STANDALONE") == "" {
   428→		// not running in standalone mode
   429→		return
   430→	}
   431→
   432→	logger, _ := zap.NewProduction()
   433→	defer logger.Sync()
   434→
   435→	cfg := DriftMonitorConfig{
   436→		Brokers:    strings.Split(getEnv("KAFKA_BROKERS", "localhost:9092"), ","),
   437→		InputTopic: getEnv("INPUT_TOPIC", "feature_distributions"),
   438→		OutputTopic: getEnv("OUTPUT_TOPIC",
   439→			"drift_events"),
   440→		GroupID:        getEnv("GROUP_ID", "drift_monitor"),
   441→		Threshold:      getEnvFloat("DRIFT_THRESHOLD", 0.2),
   442→		Logger:         logger,
   443→		CommitInterval: 3 * time.Second,
   444→	}
   445→	monitor, err := NewDriftMonitor(cfg)
   446→	if err != nil {
   447→		logger.Fatal("failed to init monitor", zap.Error(err))
   448→	}
   449→
   450→	ctx := context.Background()
   451→	if err := monitor.Run(ctx); err != nil {
   452→		logger.Fatal("monitor exited with error", zap.Error(err))
   453→	}
   454→}
   455→
   456→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   457→// Helper functions for env vars
   458→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   459→
   460→func getEnv(key, fallback string) string {
   461→	if v, ok := os.LookupEnv(key); ok {
   462→		return v
   463→	}
   464→	return fallback
   465→}
   466→
   467→func getEnvFloat(key string, fallback float64) float64 {
   468→	if v, ok := os.LookupEnv(key); ok {
   469→		if f, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64); err == nil {
   470→			return f
   471→		}
   472→	}
   473→	return fallback
   474→}

skip or request baseline?\n\t\tm.logger.Warn(\"no baseline found; skipping\",\n\t\t\tzap.String(\"model\", ev.ModelID),\n\t\t\tzap.String(\"feature\", ev.FeatureName))\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tbaseline := baseRaw.([]int64)\n\n\tpsi, err := m.driftMetric(baseline, ev.BucketCounts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tm.psiGauge.WithLabelValues(ev.ModelID, ev.FeatureName).Set(psi)\n\tm.evProcessed.Add(1)\n\n\tif psi >= m.cfg.Threshold {\n\t\tm.evDrifted.Add(1)\n\t\tm.driftCounter.WithLabelValues(ev.ModelID, ev.FeatureName).Inc()\n\n\t\tdrift := DriftEvent{\n\t\t\tModelID:     ev.ModelID,\n\t\t\tFeatureName: ev.FeatureName,\n\t\t\tMetric:      \"PSI\",\n\t\t\tScore:       psi,\n\t\t\tThreshold:   m.cfg.Threshold,\n\t\t\tTimestamp:   time.Now().UnixMilli(),\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn m.publishDriftEvent(ctx, drift)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *DriftMonitor) publishDriftEvent(ctx context.Context, ev DriftEvent) error {\n\tpayload, err := json.Marshal(ev)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tmsg := &sarama.ProducerMessage{\n\t\tTopic: m.cfg.OutputTopic,\n\t\tValue: sarama.ByteEncoder(payload),\n\t\tKey:   sarama.StringEncoder(ev.ModelID + \"|\" + ev.FeatureName),\n\t}\n\n\toperation := func() error {\n\t\t_, _, err := m.producer.SendMessage(msg)\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tnotify := func(err error, t time.Duration) {\n\t\tm.logger.Warn(\"failed to publish drift event; retrying\",\n\t\t\tzap.Error(err), zap.Duration(\"next_retry_in\", t))\n\t}\n\n\tbackoffCfg := backoff.NewExponentialBackOff()\n\tbackoffCfg.MaxElapsedTime = 30 * time.Second\n\n\treturn backoff.RetryNotify(operation, backoffCfg, notify)\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Drift Metric Implementations\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// populationStabilityIndex computes PSI using baseline and observed buckets.\n// Formula: PSI = Σ ((obs_i – base_i) * ln(obs_i / base_i))\nfunc populationStabilityIndex(baseline, observed []int64) (float64, error) {\n\tif len(baseline) != len(observed) {\n\t\treturn 0, errors.New(\"bucket length mismatch\")\n\t}\n\ttotalBase := sumInt64(baseline)\n\ttotalObs := sumInt64(observed)\n\tif totalBase == 0 || totalObs == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0, errors.New(\"empty distributions\")\n\t}\n\n\tvar psi float64\n\tfor i := range baseline {\n\t\tbaseRatio := float64(baseline[i]) / float64(totalBase)\n\t\tobsRatio := float64(observed[i]) / float64(totalObs)\n\n\t\t// apply Laplace smoothing to avoid zero ratios\n\t\tif baseRatio == 0 {\n\t\t\tbaseRatio = 0.0001\n\t\t}\n\t\tif obsRatio == 0 {\n\t\t\tobsRatio = 0.0001\n\t\t}\n\t\tpsi += (obsRatio - baseRatio) * math.Log(obsRatio/baseRatio)\n\t}\n\treturn psi, nil\n}\n\nfunc sumInt64(xs []int64) int64 {\n\tvar s int64\n\tfor _, v := range xs {\n\t\ts += v\n\t}\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper: Simple bootstrap when executed as a standalone binary.\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// When this file is built as `drift-monitor` cmd, we allow running directly\n// (useful in local docker-compose). For library use, this `main` will be\n// trimmed by the linker if not referenced.\nfunc main() {\n\tif os.Getenv(\"ECHOPULSE_STANDALONE\") == \"\" {\n\t\t// not running in standalone mode\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tlogger, _ := zap.NewProduction()\n\tdefer logger.Sync()\n\n\tcfg := DriftMonitorConfig{\n\t\tBrokers:    strings.Split(getEnv(\"KAFKA_BROKERS\", \"localhost:9092\"), \",\"),\n\t\tInputTopic: getEnv(\"INPUT_TOPIC\", \"feature_distributions\"),\n\t\tOutputTopic: getEnv(\"OUTPUT_TOPIC\",\n\t\t\t\"drift_events\"),\n\t\tGroupID:        getEnv(\"GROUP_ID\", \"drift_monitor\"),\n\t\tThreshold:      getEnvFloat(\"DRIFT_THRESHOLD\", 0.2),\n\t\tLogger:         logger,\n\t\tCommitInterval: 3 * time.Second,\n\t}\n\tmonitor, err := NewDriftMonitor(cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.Fatal(\"failed to init monitor\", zap.Error(err))\n\t}\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tif err := monitor.Run(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.Fatal(\"monitor exited with error\", zap.Error(err))\n\t}\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper functions for env vars\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunc getEnv(key, fallback string) string {\n\tif v, ok := os.LookupEnv(key); ok {\n\t\treturn v\n\t}\n\treturn fallback\n}\n\nfunc getEnvFloat(key string, fallback float64) float64 {\n\tif v, ok := os.LookupEnv(key); ok {\n\t\tif f, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64); err == nil {\n\t\t\treturn f\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn fallback\n}\n```", "numLines": 475, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 475}}

     2→// File: src/module_18.go
     3→//
     4→// Package processor contains high-throughput, pluggable NLP pipelines that fan-out from the
     5→// Social-Event bus.  This module focuses on real-time toxicity classification and demonstrates
     6→// how EchoPulse composes the Observer, Strategy and Pipeline patterns while embracing Go’s
     7→// powerful concurrency primitives.
     8→package processor
     9→
    10→import (
    11→	"bufio"
    12→	"context"
    13→	"errors"
    14→	"log"
    15→	"os"
    16→	"os/signal"
    17→	"strings"
    18→	"sync"
    19→	"syscall"
    20→	"time"
    21→
    22→	"github.com/segmentio/kafka-go"
    23→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
    24→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promauto"
    25→	"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup"
    26→)
    27→
    28→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→// DOMAIN TYPES -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31→
    32→// SocialEvent is the canonical message shape on the EchoPulse event bus.
    33→// In the production code-base this lives in a shared `domain` package, re-defined
    34→// here for standalone compilation.
    35→type SocialEvent struct {
    36→	ID        string            `json:"id"`
    37→	UserID    string            `json:"user_id"`
    38→	Timestamp time.Time         `json:"ts"`
    39→	Channel   string            `json:"channel"` // e.g. #general, @streamer, /voice-room/42
    40→	Payload   string            `json:"payload"` // raw textual content
    41→	Meta      map[string]string `json:"meta"`    // arbitrary kv pairs (e.g. language hints)
    42→}
    43→
    44→// ModerationEvent is emitted whenever a SocialEvent is deemed toxic/unsafe.
    45→// It is consumed by downstream moderation bots or dashboards.
    46→type ModerationEvent struct {
    47→	EventID      string    `json:"event_id"`
    48→	UserID       string    `json:"user_id"`
    49→	Channel      string    `json:"channel"`
    50→	ToxicityScore float64   `json:"toxicity_score"`
    51→	IsToxic      bool      `json:"is_toxic"`
    52→	CreatedAt    time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    53→	Reason       string    `json:"reason"`
    54→}
    55→
    56→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    57→// STRATEGY PATTERN: Toxicity Classifiers ---------------------------------------------------
    58→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    59→
    60→// ClassifierStrategy encapsulates an arbitrary NLP model or heuristic that can
    61→// assign a toxicity score to a SocialEvent.
    62→type ClassifierStrategy interface {
    63→	// Name returns a human-friendly identifier, used for metrics and logging.
    64→	Name() string
    65→	// Classify returns a score within [0,1] along with a binary toxic flag.
    66→	Classify(ctx context.Context, evt SocialEvent) (score float64, isToxic bool, err error)
    67→}
    68→
    69→// RuleBasedClassifier is a fast, deterministic strategy that flags events
    70→// containing words from a configurable deny-list.
    71→type RuleBasedClassifier struct {
    72→	denyList map[string]struct{}
    73→	threshold float64
    74→}
    75→
    76→// NewRuleBasedClassifier builds a RuleBasedClassifier from a newline-delimited
    77→// file containing disallowed words/phrases.  If path == "", an embedded default
    78→// list is used.
    79→func NewRuleBasedClassifier(path string, threshold float64) (*RuleBasedClassifier, error) {
    80→	var (
    81→		deny = make(map[string]struct{})
    82→		err  error
    83→	)
    84→	load := func(r *bufio.Scanner) {
    85→		for r.Scan() {
    86→			line := strings.TrimSpace(r.Text())
    87→			if line != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(line, "#") {
    88→				deny[strings.ToLower(line)] = struct{}{}
    89→			}
    90→		}
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	if path == "" {
    94→		// Built-in minimal list.
    95→		defaultWords := []string{"idiot", "stupid", "moron"}
    96→		for _, w := range defaultWords {
    97→			deny[w] = struct{}{}
    98→		}
    99→	} else {
   100→		f, e := os.Open(path)
   101→		if e != nil {
   102→			return nil, e
   103→		}
   104→		defer f.Close()
   105→		load(bufio.NewScanner(f))
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	return &RuleBasedClassifier{denyList: deny, threshold: threshold}, err
   109→}
   110→
   111→// Name implements ClassifierStrategy.
   112→func (r *RuleBasedClassifier) Name() string { return "rule_based_v1" }
   113→
   114→// Classify implements ClassifierStrategy.
   115→func (r *RuleBasedClassifier) Classify(_ context.Context, evt SocialEvent) (float64, bool, error) {
   116→	// Simple heuristic: toxicity score = (#denyWords)/(totalWords).
   117→	if evt.Payload == "" {
   118→		return 0, false, errors.New("payload empty")
   119→	}
   120→	words := strings.Fields(strings.ToLower(evt.Payload))
   121→	if len(words) == 0 {
   122→		return 0, false, errors.New("payload contained no tokenizable words")
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	var hits int
   126→	for _, w := range words {
   127→		if _, ok := r.denyList[w]; ok {
   128→			hits++
   129→		}
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	score := float64(hits) / float64(len(words))
   133→	return score, score >= r.threshold, nil
   134→}
   135→
   136→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   137→// KAFKA OBSERVER: Consumer & Producer ------------------------------------------------------
   138→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   139→
   140→// KafkaConfig bundles connection details for both consumer & producer.
   141→type KafkaConfig struct {
   142→	Brokers     []string
   143→	GroupID     string
   144→	ConsumeTopic string
   145→	ProduceTopic string
   146→}
   147→
   148→// NewConsumer returns a configured Kafka reader.
   149→func NewConsumer(cfg KafkaConfig) *kafka.Reader {
   150→	return kafka.NewReader(kafka.ReaderConfig{
   151→		Brokers: cfg.Brokers,
   152→		GroupID: cfg.GroupID,
   153→		Topic:   cfg.ConsumeTopic,
   154→		// Tune for high throughput / low latency
   155→		MinBytes: 10e3, // 10KB
   156→		MaxBytes: 10e6, // 10MB
   157→	})
   158→}
   159→
   160→// NewProducer returns a Kafka writer for moderation events.
   161→func NewProducer(cfg KafkaConfig) *kafka.Writer {
   162→	return &kafka.Writer{
   163→		Addr:         kafka.TCP(cfg.Brokers...),
   164→		Topic:        cfg.ProduceTopic,
   165→		Balancer:     &kafka.LeastBytes{},
   166→		RequiredAcks: kafka.RequireAll,
   167→		Async:        false,
   168→	}
   169→}
   170→
   171→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→// METRICS ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   173→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   174→
   175→var (
   176→	eventsConsumed = promauto.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
   177→		Name: "echopulse_toxicity_events_consumed_total",
   178→		Help: "Total number of social events consumed by toxicity service.",
   179→	}, []string{"channel"})
   180→
   181→	eventsProduced = promauto.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
   182→		Name: "echopulse_toxicity_events_produced_total",
   183→		Help: "Total number of moderation events produced by toxicity service.",
   184→	}, []string{"channel", "toxic"})
   185→
   186→	classifierErrors = promauto.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
   187→		Name: "echopulse_toxicity_classifier_errors_total",
   188→		Help: "Total classification errors by strategy.",
   189→	}, []string{"strategy"})
   190→)
   191→
   192→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   193→// PIPELINE RUNNER -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   194→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   195→
   196→// ToxicityService wires together a Kafka consumer, a producer, a classifier
   197→// strategy, and a worker-pool to perform near-real-time moderation scoring.
   198→type ToxicityService struct {
   199→	cfg        KafkaConfig
   200→	consumer   *kafka.Reader
   201→	producer   *kafka.Writer
   202→	classifier ClassifierStrategy
   203→	workers    int
   204→}
   205→
   206→// NewToxicityService is the constructor.
   207→func NewToxicityService(cfg KafkaConfig, classifier ClassifierStrategy, workers int) *ToxicityService {
   208→	return &ToxicityService{
   209→		cfg:        cfg,
   210→		consumer:   NewConsumer(cfg),
   211→		producer:   NewProducer(cfg),
   212→		classifier: classifier,
   213→		workers:    workers,
   214→	}
   215→}
   216→
   217→// Run blocks until ctx is canceled or fatal error occurs.
   218→func (s *ToxicityService) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
   219→	g, ctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)
   220→
   221→	// Fan-out worker goroutines reading from shared consumer.
   222→	for i := 0; i < s.workers; i++ {
   223→		g.Go(func() error {
   224→			for {
   225→				m, err := s.consumer.FetchMessage(ctx)
   226→				if err != nil {
   227→					// context canceled?
   228→					if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   229→						return nil
   230→					}
   231→					return err
   232→				}
   233→
   234→				var evt SocialEvent
   235→				if err := jsonUnmarshal(m.Value, &evt); err != nil {
   236→					log.Printf("WARN: invalid payload: %v", err)
   237→					// commit offset anyway to skip bad message.
   238→					_ = s.consumer.CommitMessages(ctx, m)
   239→					continue
   240→				}
   241→
   242→				eventsConsumed.WithLabelValues(evt.Channel).Inc()
   243→				score, toxic, err := s.classifier.Classify(ctx, evt)
   244→				if err != nil {
   245→					classifierErrors.WithLabelValues(s.classifier.Name()).Inc()
   246→					log.Printf("ERROR: classification failed: %v", err)
   247→					// Do NOT commit offset; we may want to retry later
   248→					continue
   249→				}
   250→
   251→				mod := ModerationEvent{
   252→					EventID:       evt.ID,
   253→					UserID:        evt.UserID,
   254→					Channel:       evt.Channel,
   255→					ToxicityScore: score,
   256→					IsToxic:       toxic,
   257→					CreatedAt:     time.Now().UTC(),
   258→					Reason:        s.classifier.Name(),
   259→				}
   260→
   261→				if err := s.publish(ctx, mod); err != nil {
   262→					log.Printf("ERROR: failed to publish moderation event: %v", err)
   263→					// do NOT commit offset to allow retry
   264→					continue
   265→				}
   266→
   267→				eventsProduced.WithLabelValues(evt.Channel, boolToString(toxic)).Inc()
   268→				_ = s.consumer.CommitMessages(ctx, m)
   269→			}
   270→		})
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	return g.Wait()
   274→}
   275→
   276→// publish sends a ModerationEvent to the Kafka producer with simple
   277→// retry/backoff logic to handle transient network partitions.
   278→func (s *ToxicityService) publish(ctx context.Context, mod ModerationEvent) error {
   279→	const (
   280→		maxRetries = 5
   281→		baseDelay  = 100 * time.Millisecond
   282→	)
   283→
   284→	payload, err := jsonMarshal(mod)
   285→	if err != nil {
   286→		return err
   287→	}
   288→
   289→	var attempt int
   290→	for {
   291→		err = s.producer.WriteMessages(ctx, kafka.Message{
   292→			Key:   []byte(mod.EventID),
   293→			Value: payload,
   294→			Time:  time.Now(),
   295→		})
   296→		if err == nil {
   297→			return nil
   298→		}
   299→
   300→		if attempt >= maxRetries {
   301→			return err
   302→		}
   303→		attempt++
   304→		sleep := baseDelay * time.Duration(1<<attempt)
   305→		select {
   306→		case <-time.After(sleep):
   307→		case <-ctx.Done():
   308→			return ctx.Err()
   309→		}
   310→	}
   311→}
   312→
   313→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   314→// BOOTSTRAP -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   315→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   316→
   317→// jsonMarshal / jsonUnmarshal wrap the standard json calls behind an alias to
   318→// facilitate future migration to a faster library (e.g. jsoniter) without
   319→// touching call-sites.
   320→func jsonMarshal(v any) ([]byte, error)   { return json.Marshal(v) }
   321→func jsonUnmarshal(data []byte, v any) error { return json.Unmarshal(data, v) }
   322→
   323→// boolToString helper.
   324→func boolToString(b bool) string {
   325→	if b {
   326→		return "true"
   327→	}
   328→	return "false"
   329→}
   330→
   331→// Entrypoint wires signals, context, and launches the toxicity service.
   332→// This file can be built as a stand-alone command via: go build -o toxicity cmd/toxicity/main.go
   333→func main() {
   334→	// Accept config from env or CLI flags in real code-base; simplified here.
   335→	cfg := KafkaConfig{
   336→		Brokers:      strings.Split(os.Getenv("KAFKA_BROKERS"), ","),
   337→		GroupID:      "echopulse-toxicity-service",
   338→		ConsumeTopic: "social.events.raw",
   339→		ProduceTopic: "moderation.events",
   340→	}
   341→
   342→	classifier, err := NewRuleBasedClassifier(os.Getenv("DENYLIST_PATH"), 0.1)
   343→	if err != nil {
   344→		log.Fatalf("failed to init classifier: %v", err)
   345→	}
   346→
   347→	svc := NewToxicityService(cfg, classifier, 8)
   348→
   349→	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   350→	defer cancel()
   351→
   352→	if err := svc.Run(ctx); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   353→		log.Fatalf("toxicity service exited: %v", err)
   354→	}
   355→}
   356→
   357→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   358→// MISC IMPORTS ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   359→// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   360→
   361→// The imports below are grouped here to centralize dependencies and avoid
   362→// linter warnings for “blank” imports used exclusively for side effects.
   363→import (
   364→	"encoding/json"
   365→)

logging.\n\tName() string\n\t// Classify returns a score within [0,1] along with a binary toxic flag.\n\tClassify(ctx context.Context, evt SocialEvent) (score float64, isToxic bool, err error)\n}\n\n// RuleBasedClassifier is a fast, deterministic strategy that flags events\n// containing words from a configurable deny-list.\ntype RuleBasedClassifier struct {\n\tdenyList map[string]struct{}\n\tthreshold float64\n}\n\n// NewRuleBasedClassifier builds a RuleBasedClassifier from a newline-delimited\n// file containing disallowed words/phrases.  If path == \"\", an embedded default\n// list is used.\nfunc NewRuleBasedClassifier(path string, threshold float64) (*RuleBasedClassifier, error) {\n\tvar (\n\t\tdeny = make(map[string]struct{})\n\t\terr  error\n\t)\n\tload := func(r *bufio.Scanner) {\n\t\tfor r.Scan() {\n\t\t\tline := strings.TrimSpace(r.Text())\n\t\t\tif line != \"\" && !strings.HasPrefix(line, \"#\") {\n\t\t\t\tdeny[strings.ToLower(line)] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\t// Built-in minimal list.\n\t\tdefaultWords := []string{\"idiot\", \"stupid\", \"moron\"}\n\t\tfor _, w := range defaultWords {\n\t\t\tdeny[w] = struct{}{}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tf, e := os.Open(path)\n\t\tif e != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, e\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer f.Close()\n\t\tload(bufio.NewScanner(f))\n\t}\n\n\treturn &RuleBasedClassifier{denyList: deny, threshold: threshold}, err\n}\n\n// Name implements ClassifierStrategy.\nfunc (r *RuleBasedClassifier) Name() string { return \"rule_based_v1\" }\n\n// Classify implements ClassifierStrategy.\nfunc (r *RuleBasedClassifier) Classify(_ context.Context, evt SocialEvent) (float64, bool, error) {\n\t// Simple heuristic: toxicity score = (#denyWords)/(totalWords).\n\tif evt.Payload == \"\" {\n\t\treturn 0, false, errors.New(\"payload empty\")\n\t}\n\twords := strings.Fields(strings.ToLower(evt.Payload))\n\tif len(words) == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0, false, errors.New(\"payload contained no tokenizable words\")\n\t}\n\n\tvar hits int\n\tfor _, w := range words {\n\t\tif _, ok := r.denyList[w]; ok {\n\t\t\thits++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tscore := float64(hits) / float64(len(words))\n\treturn score, score >= r.threshold, nil\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// KAFKA OBSERVER: Consumer & Producer ------------------------------------------------------\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// KafkaConfig bundles connection details for both consumer & producer.\ntype KafkaConfig struct {\n\tBrokers     []string\n\tGroupID     string\n\tConsumeTopic string\n\tProduceTopic string\n}\n\n// NewConsumer returns a configured Kafka reader.\nfunc NewConsumer(cfg KafkaConfig) *kafka.Reader {\n\treturn kafka.NewReader(kafka.ReaderConfig{\n\t\tBrokers: cfg.Brokers,\n\t\tGroupID: cfg.GroupID,\n\t\tTopic:   cfg.ConsumeTopic,\n\t\t// Tune for high throughput / low latency\n\t\tMinBytes: 10e3, // 10KB\n\t\tMaxBytes: 10e6, // 10MB\n\t})\n}\n\n// NewProducer returns a Kafka writer for moderation events.\nfunc NewProducer(cfg KafkaConfig) *kafka.Writer {\n\treturn &kafka.Writer{\n\t\tAddr:         kafka.TCP(cfg.Brokers...),\n\t\tTopic:        cfg.ProduceTopic,\n\t\tBalancer:     &kafka.LeastBytes{},\n\t\tRequiredAcks: kafka.RequireAll,\n\t\tAsync:        false,\n\t}\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// METRICS ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nvar (\n\teventsConsumed = promauto.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{\n\t\tName: \"echopulse_toxicity_events_consumed_total\",\n\t\tHelp: \"Total number of social events consumed by toxicity service.\",\n\t}, []string{\"channel\"})\n\n\teventsProduced = promauto.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{\n\t\tName: \"echopulse_toxicity_events_produced_total\",\n\t\tHelp: \"Total number of moderation events produced by toxicity service.\",\n\t}, []string{\"channel\", \"toxic\"})\n\n\tclassifierErrors = promauto.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{\n\t\tName: \"echopulse_toxicity_classifier_errors_total\",\n\t\tHelp: \"Total classification errors by strategy.\",\n\t}, []string{\"strategy\"})\n)\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// PIPELINE RUNNER -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// ToxicityService wires together a Kafka consumer, a producer, a classifier\n// strategy, and a worker-pool to perform near-real-time moderation scoring.\ntype ToxicityService struct {\n\tcfg        KafkaConfig\n\tconsumer   *kafka.Reader\n\tproducer   *kafka.Writer\n\tclassifier ClassifierStrategy\n\tworkers    int\n}\n\n// NewToxicityService is the constructor.\nfunc NewToxicityService(cfg KafkaConfig, classifier ClassifierStrategy, workers int) *ToxicityService {\n\treturn &ToxicityService{\n\t\tcfg:        cfg,\n\t\tconsumer:   NewConsumer(cfg),\n\t\tproducer:   NewProducer(cfg),\n\t\tclassifier: classifier,\n\t\tworkers:    workers,\n\t}\n}\n\n// Run blocks until ctx is canceled or fatal error occurs.\nfunc (s *ToxicityService) Run(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tg, ctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n\n\t// Fan-out worker goroutines reading from shared consumer.\n\tfor i := 0; i < s.workers; i++ {\n\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\t\tfor {\n\t\t\t\tm, err := s.consumer.FetchMessage(ctx)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t// context canceled?\n\t\t\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tvar evt SocialEvent\n\t\t\t\tif err := jsonUnmarshal(m.Value, &evt); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"WARN: invalid payload: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t// commit offset anyway to skip bad message.\n\t\t\t\t\t_ = s.consumer.CommitMessages(ctx, m)\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\teventsConsumed.WithLabelValues(evt.Channel).Inc()\n\t\t\t\tscore, toxic, err := s.classifier.Classify(ctx, evt)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tclassifierErrors.WithLabelValues(s.classifier.Name()).Inc()\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"ERROR: classification failed: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t// Do NOT commit offset; we may want to retry later\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tmod := ModerationEvent{\n\t\t\t\t\tEventID:       evt.ID,\n\t\t\t\t\tUserID:        evt.UserID,\n\t\t\t\t\tChannel:       evt.Channel,\n\t\t\t\t\tToxicityScore: score,\n\t\t\t\t\tIsToxic:       toxic,\n\t\t\t\t\tCreatedAt:     time.Now().UTC(),\n\t\t\t\t\tReason:        s.classifier.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif err := s.publish(ctx, mod); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"ERROR: failed to publish moderation event: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t// do NOT commit offset to allow retry\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\teventsProduced.WithLabelValues(evt.Channel, boolToString(toxic)).Inc()\n\t\t\t\t_ = s.consumer.CommitMessages(ctx, m)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn g.Wait()\n}\n\n// publish sends a ModerationEvent to the Kafka producer with simple\n// retry/backoff logic to handle transient network partitions.\nfunc (s *ToxicityService) publish(ctx context.Context, mod ModerationEvent) error {\n\tconst (\n\t\tmaxRetries = 5\n\t\tbaseDelay  = 100 * time.Millisecond\n\t)\n\n\tpayload, err := jsonMarshal(mod)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tvar attempt int\n\tfor {\n\t\terr = s.producer.WriteMessages(ctx, kafka.Message{\n\t\t\tKey:   []byte(mod.EventID),\n\t\t\tValue: payload,\n\t\t\tTime:  time.Now(),\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif attempt >= maxRetries {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t\tattempt++\n\t\tsleep := baseDelay * time.Duration(1<<attempt)\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-time.After(sleep):\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn ctx.Err()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// BOOTSTRAP error)   { return json.Marshal(v) }\nfunc jsonUnmarshal(data []byte, v any) error { return json.Unmarshal(data, v) }\n\n// boolToString helper.\nfunc boolToString(b bool) string {\n\tif b {\n\t\treturn \"true\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"false\"\n}\n\n// Entrypoint wires signals, context, and launches the toxicity service.\n// This file can be built as a stand-alone command via: go build -o      2→// File: src/module_5.go
     3→//
     4→// EchoPulse – Real-Time Social Signal Processing Platform (ml_nlp)
     5→// -----------------------------------------------------------------
     6→// Module 5: Online Feature-Drift Detection & Alerting
     7→//
     8→// This module contains a generic, online drift–detection component
     9→// that consumes live feature vectors, compares their distributions
    10→// against a (versioned) training baseline, and emits `DriftEvent`s
    11→// to the platform event-bus when statistically significant drift is
    12→// observed.  The implementation demonstrates several architectural
    13→// patterns used across EchoPulse:
    14→//
    15→//   • Strategy Pattern      – interchangeable statistical tests
    16→//   • Factory  Pattern      – test constructor registry
    17→//   • Observer Pattern      – subscription to a live feature stream
    18→//   • Pipeline  Pattern     – windowed aggregation + decision stage
    19→//
    20→// It is intentionally self-contained and uses only light external
    21→// dependencies (gonum for statistics and segmentio/kafka-go for the
    22→// message bus publisher).  The code can be dropped into a larger
    23→// code-base or run standalone for demonstration purposes.
    24→//
    25→// -----------------------------------------------------------------
    26→
    27→package monitor // import "github.com/echopulse/ep/monitor"
    28→
    29→import (
    30→	"context"
    31→	"encoding/json"
    32→	"errors"
    33→	"fmt"
    34→	"math"
    35→	"sort"
    36→	"sync"
    37→	"time"
    38→
    39→	kafka "github.com/segmentio/kafka-go"
    40→	"gonum.org/v1/gonum/stat"
    41→)
    42→
    43→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44→// Public Domain Types
    45→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46→
    47→// FeatureVector represents a canonicalised, numeric feature vector
    48→// generated by an upstream pipeline stage.  Non-numeric features
    49→// should be pre-encoded before reaching the detector.
    50→type FeatureVector map[string]float64
    51→
    52→// DriftEvent is raised whenever the detector concludes that the
    53→// distribution of a feature in the live stream has drifted away from
    54→// the baseline with statistical significance lower than Alpha.
    55→type DriftEvent struct {
    56→	Timestamp    time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
    57→	Feature      string    `json:"feature"`
    58→	Test         string    `json:"test"`
    59→	PValue       float64   `json:"p_value"`
    60→	WindowSize   int       `json:"window_size"`
    61→	BaselineSize int       `json:"baseline_size"`
    62→	ModelVer     string    `json:"model_version"`
    63→}
    64→
    65→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    66→// Strategy Pattern → Statistical Test Interface
    67→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    68→
    69→// StatisticalTest abstracts a two-sample test that returns the
    70→// p-value for the null-hypothesis "samples originate from the same
    71→// distribution".
    72→type StatisticalTest interface {
    73→	PValue(sampleA, sampleB []float64) (float64, error)
    74→	Name() string
    75→}
    76→
    77→// registry maps symbolic names onto factory functions so that tests
    78→// can be configured in TOML/YAML/etc. at runtime.
    79→var registry = map[string]func() StatisticalTest{
    80→	"ks":     func() StatisticalTest { return &KSTest{} },
    81→	"mannwh": func() StatisticalTest { return &MannWhitneyUTest{} },
    82→}
    83→
    84→// GetTest returns an initialised StatisticalTest by name.
    85→func GetTest(name string) (StatisticalTest, error) {
    86→	f, ok := registry[name]
    87→	if !ok {
    88→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown statistical test %q", name)
    89→	}
    90→	return f(), nil
    91→}
    92→
    93→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    94→// Concrete Strategies
    95→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    96→
    97→// KSTest – two-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov (continuous features).
    98→type KSTest struct{}
    99→
   100→func (KSTest) Name() string { return "ks" }
   101→
   102→func (KSTest) PValue(a, b []float64) (float64, error) {
   103→	if len(a) < 2 || len(b) < 2 {
   104→		return 1.0, errors.New("insufficient sample size for ks test")
   105→	}
   106→	statistic := ksStatistic(a, b)
   107→	p := ksAsymptoticP(statistic, len(a), len(b))
   108→	return p, nil
   109→}
   110→
   111→// Mann-Whitney-U (a.k.a. Wilcoxon Rank-Sum) – robust to outliers.
   112→type MannWhitneyUTest struct{}
   113→
   114→func (MannWhitneyUTest) Name() string { return "mannwh" }
   115→
   116→func (MannWhitneyUTest) PValue(a, b []float64) (float64, error) {
   117→	if len(a) < 2 || len(b) < 2 {
   118→		return 1.0, errors.New("insufficient sample size for mann-whitney test")
   119→	}
   120→	p := stat.MannWhitneyUTest(a, b, nil)
   121→	return p, nil
   122→}
   123→
   124→// ksStatistic computes the two-sample KS-D statistic.
   125→func ksStatistic(x, y []float64) float64 {
   126→	xs := append([]float64(nil), x...)
   127→	ys := append([]float64(nil), y...)
   128→	sort.Float64s(xs)
   129→	sort.Float64s(ys)
   130→
   131→	nx, ny := len(xs), len(ys)
   132→	i, j := 0, 0
   133→	var dMax float64
   134→	for i < nx && j < ny {
   135→		vx, vy := xs[i], ys[j]
   136→		switch {
   137→		case vx <= vy:
   138→			i++
   139→		case vy < vx:
   140→			j++
   141→		}
   142→		cdfX := float64(i) / float64(nx)
   143→		cdfY := float64(j) / float64(ny)
   144→		diff := math.Abs(cdfX - cdfY)
   145→		if diff > dMax {
   146→			dMax = diff
   147→		}
   148→	}
   149→	return dMax
   150→}
   151→
   152→// ksAsymptoticP calculates the asymptotic KS p-value.
   153→func ksAsymptoticP(d float64, n, m int) float64 {
   154→	ne := float64(n*m) / float64(n+m)
   155→	lambda := (math.Sqrt(ne) + 0.12 + 0.11/math.Sqrt(ne)) * d
   156→	// 2 * Σ (-1)^{k-1} exp(-2 k² λ²)  (k from 1..∞).  Truncate at 5 terms.
   157→	var sum float64
   158→	for k := 1; k <= 5; k++ {
   159→		term := math.Exp(-2 * float64(k*k) * lambda * lambda)
   160→		if k%2 == 1 {
   161→			sum += term
   162→		} else {
   163→			sum -= term
   164→		}
   165→	}
   166→	return math.Max(math.Min(2*sum, 1.0), 0.0)
   167→}
   168→
   169→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   170→// EventBus Abstraction + Kafka Publisher Implementation
   171→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→
   173→// EventBus is deliberately minimal.  In production EchoPulse uses a
   174→// richer contract (ACKS, partitions, JetStream, etc.).
   175→type EventBus interface {
   176→	Publish(ctx context.Context, topic string, msg []byte) error
   177→}
   178→
   179→type KafkaBus struct {
   180→	w *kafka.Writer
   181→}
   182→
   183→func NewKafkaBus(brokers []string) *KafkaBus {
   184→	return &KafkaBus{
   185→		w: &kafka.Writer{
   186→			Addr:         kafka.TCP(brokers...),
   187→			RequiredAcks: kafka.RequireAll,
   188→			Async:        true,
   189→		},
   190→	}
   191→}
   192→
   193→func (k *KafkaBus) Publish(ctx context.Context, topic string, msg []byte) error {
   194→	return k.w.WriteMessages(ctx, kafka.Message{
   195→		Topic: topic,
   196→		Value: msg,
   197→		Time:  time.Now().UTC(),
   198→	})
   199→}
   200→
   201→func (k *KafkaBus) Close() error { return k.w.Close() }
   202→
   203→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   204→// DriftDetector – Core Online Detection Engine
   205→// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   206→
   207→// DriftDetector is goroutine-safe.
   208→type DriftDetector struct {
   209→	baseline   map[string][]float64 // immutable after init
   210→	window     []FeatureVector
   211→	maxWindow  int
   212→	alpha      float64
   213→	modelVer   string
   214→	test       StatisticalTest
   215→	bus        EventBus
   216→	busTopic   string
   217→	mu         sync.Mutex
   218→	cancelLoop context.CancelFunc
   219→}
   220→
   221→// DriftDetectorConfig provides dependency-injection hooks.
   222→type DriftDetectorConfig struct {
   223→	Baseline    map[string][]float64 // e.g. pulled from feature store
   224→	Test        string               // "ks" or "mannwh" …
   225→	WindowSize  int                  // sliding window N
   226→	Alpha       float64              // significance threshold
   227→	ModelVer    string               // baseline model version
   228→	Bus         EventBus
   229→	BusTopic    string
   230→}
   231→
   232→// NewDriftDetector validates the config and builds a detector.
   233→func NewDriftDetector(cfg DriftDetectorConfig) (*DriftDetector, error) {
   234→	if cfg.WindowSize < 10 {
   235→		return nil, errors.New("window size must be ≥ 10")
   236→	}
   237→	if cfg.Alpha <= 0 || cfg.Alpha >= 1 {
   238→		return nil, errors.New("alpha must lie in (0,1)")
   239→	}
   240→	test, err := GetTest(cfg.Test)
   241→	if err != nil {
   242→		return nil, err
   243→	}
   244→	if cfg.Bus == nil {
   245→		return nil, errors.New("event-bus may not be nil")
   246→	}
   247→	return &DriftDetector{
   248→		baseline:  cfg.Baseline,
   249→		maxWindow: cfg.WindowSize,
   250→		alpha:     cfg.Alpha,
   251→		modelVer:  cfg.ModelVer,
   252→		test:      test,
   253→		bus:       cfg.Bus,
   254→		busTopic:  cfg.BusTopic,
   255→	}, nil
   256→}
   257→
   258→// Observe enqueues a new FeatureVector and (asynchronously) performs
   259→// drift checks when the sliding window is full.
   260→func (d *DriftDetector) Observe(vec FeatureVector) {
   261→	d.mu.Lock()
   262→	defer d.mu.Unlock()
   263→
   264→	// Append sample
   265→	d.window = append(d.window, vec)
   266→	if len(d.window) > d.maxWindow {
   267→		d.window = d.window[1:] // pop front
   268→	}
   269→
   270→	// Spawn test in a worker-go-routine so Observe stays low-latency
   271→	if len(d.window) == d.maxWindow {
   272→		snapshot := make([]FeatureVector, len(d.window))
   273→		copy(snapshot, d.window)
   274→
   275→		go d.checkWindow(snapshot)
   276→	}
   277→}
   278→
   279→// checkWindow aggregates by feature & applies the selected test.
   280→func (d *DriftDetector) checkWindow(window []FeatureVector) {
   281→	// Aggregate incoming window into per-feature slices
   282→	buffers := map[string][]float64{}
   283→	for _, vec := range window {
   284→		for k, v := range vec {
   285→			buffers[k] = append(buffers[k], v)
   286→		}
   287→	}
   288→
   289→	// Run tests for each feature
   290→	for feat, sample := range buffers {
   291→		base, ok := d.baseline[feat]
   292→		if !ok || len(base) < 10 {
   293→			// No/bad baseline – skip; in prod log a warning
   294→			continue
   295→		}
   296→		p, err := d.test.PValue(sample, base)
   297→		if err != nil {
   298→			continue
   299→		}
   300→		if p < d.alpha {
   301→			d.emit(feat, p, len(sample), len(base))
   302→		}
   303→	}
   304→}
   305→
   306→// emit publishes a DriftEvent as JSON to the bus.
   307→func (d *DriftDetector) emit(feature string, p float64, win, base int) {
   308→	evt := DriftEvent{
   309→		Timestamp:    time.Now().UTC(),
   310→		Feature:      feature,
   311→		Test:         d.test.Name(),
   312→		PValue:       p,
   313→		WindowSize:   win,
   314→		BaselineSize: base,
   315→		ModelVer:     d.modelVer,
   316→	}
   317→	payload, _ := json.Marshal(evt) // cannot fail for serialisable struct
   318→	_ = d.bus.Publish(context.TODO(), d.busTopic, payload)
   319→}
   320→
   321→// Run starts a background watchdog that periodically rotates the
   322→// baseline from an injected callback (stubbed here for brevity).
   323→// This demonstrates how the detector can be kept fresh without
   324→// tearing down producers/consumers.
   325→//
   326→//	c := detector.Run(ctx, 24*time.Hour, fetchNewBaseline)
   327→//
   328→// The returned channel is closed once the rotation loop ends.
   329→func (d *DriftDetector) Run(
   330→	parent context.Context,
   331→	interval time.Duration,
   332→	refresh func() (map[string][]float64, string, error),
   333→) <-chan struct{} {
   334→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(parent)
   335→	d.cancelLoop = cancel
   336→	done := make(chan struct{})
   337→
   338→	go func() {

The implementation demonstrates several architectural\n// patterns used across EchoPulse:\n//\n//   • Strategy Pattern      – interchangeable statistical tests\n//   • Factory  Pattern      – test constructor registry\n//   • Observer Pattern      – subscription to a live feature stream\n//   • Pipeline  Pattern     – windowed aggregation + decision stage\n//\n// It is intentionally self-contained and uses only light external\n// dependencies (gonum for statistics and segmentio/kafka-go for the\n// message bus publisher).  The code can be dropped into a larger\n// code-base or run standalone for demonstration purposes.\n//\n// -----------------------------------------------------------------\n\npackage monitor // import \"github.com/echopulse/ep/monitor\"\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tkafka \"github.com/segmentio/kafka-go\"\n\t\"gonum.org/v1/gonum/stat\"\n)\n\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Public Domain Types\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// FeatureVector represents a canonicalised, numeric feature vector\n// generated by an upstream pipeline stage.  Non-numeric features\n// should be pre-encoded before reaching the detector.\ntype FeatureVector map[string]float64\n\n// DriftEvent is raised whenever the detector concludes that the\n// distribution of a feature in the live stream has drifted away from\n// the baseline with statistical significance lower than Alpha.\ntype DriftEvent struct {\n\tTimestamp    time.Time `json:\"timestamp\"`\n\tFeature      string    `json:\"feature\"`\n\tTest         string    `json:\"test\"`\n\tPValue       float64   `json:\"p_value\"`\n\tWindowSize   int       `json:\"window_size\"`\n\tBaselineSize int       `json:\"baseline_size\"`\n\tModelVer     string    `json:\"model_version\"`\n}\n\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Strategy Pattern → Statistical Test Interface\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// StatisticalTest abstracts a two-sample test that returns the\n// p-value for the null-hypothesis \"samples originate from the same\n// distribution\".\ntype StatisticalTest interface {\n\tPValue(sampleA, sampleB []float64) (float64, error)\n\tName() string\n}\n\n// registry maps symbolic names onto factory functions so that tests\n// can be configured in TOML/YAML/etc. at runtime.\nvar registry = map[string]func() StatisticalTest{\n\t\"ks\":     func() StatisticalTest { return &KSTest{} },\n\t\"mannwh\": func() StatisticalTest { return &MannWhitneyUTest{} },\n}\n\n// GetTest returns an initialised StatisticalTest by name.\nfunc GetTest(name string) (StatisticalTest, error) {\n\tf, ok := registry[name]\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unknown statistical test %q\", name)\n\t}\n\treturn f(), nil\n}\n\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Concrete Strategies\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// KSTest – two-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov (continuous features).\ntype KSTest struct{}\n\nfunc (KSTest) Name() string { return \"ks\" }\n\nfunc (KSTest) PValue(a, b []float64) (float64, error) {\n\tif len(a) < 2 || len(b) < 2 {\n\t\treturn 1.0, errors.New(\"insufficient sample size for ks test\")\n\t}\n\tstatistic := ksStatistic(a, b)\n\tp := ksAsymptoticP(statistic, len(a), len(b))\n\treturn p, nil\n}\n\n// Mann-Whitney-U (a.k.a. Wilcoxon Rank-Sum) – robust to outliers.\ntype MannWhitneyUTest struct{}\n\nfunc (MannWhitneyUTest) Name() string { return \"mannwh\" }\n\nfunc (MannWhitneyUTest) PValue(a, b []float64) (float64, error) {\n\tif len(a) < 2 || len(b) < 2 {\n\t\treturn 1.0, errors.New(\"insufficient sample size for mann-whitney test\")\n\t}\n\tp := stat.MannWhitneyUTest(a, b, nil)\n\treturn p, nil\n}\n\n// ksStatistic computes the two-sample KS-D statistic.\nfunc ksStatistic(x, y []float64) float64 {\n\txs := append([]float64(nil), x...)\n\tys := append([]float64(nil), y...)\n\tsort.Float64s(xs)\n\tsort.Float64s(ys)\n\n\tnx, ny := len(xs), len(ys)\n\ti, j := 0, 0\n\tvar dMax float64\n\tfor i < nx && j < ny {\n\t\tvx, vy := xs[i], ys[j]\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase vx <= vy:\n\t\t\ti++\n\t\tcase vy < vx:\n\t\t\tj++\n\t\t}\n\t\tcdfX := float64(i) / float64(nx)\n\t\tcdfY := float64(j) / float64(ny)\n\t\tdiff := math.Abs(cdfX - cdfY)\n\t\tif diff > dMax {\n\t\t\tdMax = diff\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn dMax\n}\n\n// ksAsymptoticP calculates the asymptotic KS p-value.\nfunc ksAsymptoticP(d float64, n, m int) float64 {\n\tne := float64(n*m) / float64(n+m)\n\tlambda := (math.Sqrt(ne) + 0.12 + 0.11/math.Sqrt(ne)) * d\n\t// 2 * Σ (-1)^{k-1} exp(-2 k² λ²)  (k from 1..∞).  Truncate at 5 terms.\n\tvar sum float64\n\tfor k := 1; k <= 5; k++ {\n\t\tterm := math.Exp(-2 * float64(k*k) * lambda * lambda)\n\t\tif k%2 == 1 {\n\t\t\tsum += term\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tsum -= term\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn math.Max(math.Min(2*sum, 1.0), 0.0)\n}\n\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// EventBus Abstraction + Kafka Publisher Implementation\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// EventBus is deliberately minimal.  In production EchoPulse uses a\n// richer contract (ACKS, partitions, JetStream, etc.).\ntype EventBus interface {\n\tPublish(ctx context.Context, topic string, msg []byte) error\n}\n\ntype KafkaBus struct {\n\tw *kafka.Writer\n}\n\nfunc NewKafkaBus(brokers []string) *KafkaBus {\n\treturn &KafkaBus{\n\t\tw: &kafka.Writer{\n\t\t\tAddr:         kafka.TCP(brokers...),\n\t\t\tRequiredAcks: kafka.RequireAll,\n\t\t\tAsync:        true,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (k *KafkaBus) Publish(ctx context.Context, topic string, msg []byte) error {\n\treturn k.w.WriteMessages(ctx, kafka.Message{\n\t\tTopic: topic,\n\t\tValue: msg,\n\t\tTime:  time.Now().UTC(),\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc (k *KafkaBus) Close() error { return k.w.Close() }\n\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DriftDetector – Core Online Detection Engine\n// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// DriftDetector is goroutine-safe.\ntype DriftDetector struct {\n\tbaseline   map[string][]float64 // immutable after init\n\twindow     []FeatureVector\n\tmaxWindow  int\n\talpha      float64\n\tmodelVer   string\n\ttest       StatisticalTest\n\tbus        EventBus\n\tbusTopic   string\n\tmu         sync.Mutex\n\tcancelLoop context.CancelFunc\n}\n\n// DriftDetectorConfig provides dependency-injection hooks.\ntype DriftDetectorConfig struct {\n\tBaseline    map[string][]float64 // e.g. pulled from feature store\n\tTest        string               // \"ks\" or \"mannwh\" …\n\tWindowSize  int                  // sliding window N\n\tAlpha       float64              // significance threshold\n\tModelVer    string               // baseline model version\n\tBus         EventBus\n\tBusTopic    string\n}\n\n// NewDriftDetector validates the config and builds a detector.\nfunc NewDriftDetector(cfg DriftDetectorConfig) (*DriftDetector, error) {\n\tif cfg.WindowSize < 10 {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"window size must be ≥ 10\")\n\t}\n\tif cfg.Alpha <= 0 || cfg.Alpha >= 1 {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"alpha must lie in (0,1)\")\n\t}\n\ttest, err := GetTest(cfg.Test)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tif cfg.Bus == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"event-bus may not be nil\")\n\t}\n\treturn &DriftDetector{\n\t\tbaseline:  cfg.Baseline,\n\t\tmaxWindow: cfg.WindowSize,\n\t\talpha:     cfg.Alpha,\n\t\tmodelVer:  cfg.ModelVer,\n\t\ttest:      test,\n\t\tbus:       cfg.Bus,\n\t\tbusTopic:  cfg.BusTopic,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// Observe enqueues a new FeatureVector and (asynchronously) performs\n// drift checks when the sliding window is full.\nfunc (d *DriftDetector) Observe(vec FeatureVector) {\n\td.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer d.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Append sample\n\td.window = append(d.window, vec)\n\tif len(d.window) > d.maxWindow {\n\t\td.window = d.window[1:] // pop front\n\t}\n\n\t// Spawn test in a worker-go-routine so Observe stays low-latency\n\tif len(d.window) == d.maxWindow {\n\t\tsnapshot := make([]FeatureVector, len(d.window))\n\t\tcopy(snapshot, d.window)\n\n\t\tgo d.checkWindow(snapshot)\n\t}\n}\n\n// checkWindow aggregates by feature & applies the selected test.\nfunc (d *DriftDetector) checkWindow(window []FeatureVector) {\n\t// Aggregate incoming window into per-feature slices\n\tbuffers := map[string][]float64{}\n\tfor _, vec := range window {\n\t\tfor k, v := range vec {\n\t\t\tbuffers[k] = append(buffers[k], v)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Run tests for each feature\n\tfor feat, sample := range buffers {\n\t\tbase, ok := d.baseline[feat]\n\t\tif !ok || len(base) < 10 {\n\t\t\t// No/bad baseline – skip; in prod log a warning\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tp, err := d.test.PValue(sample, base)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif p < d.alpha {\n\t\t\td.emit(feat, p, len(sample), len(base))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// emit publishes a DriftEvent as JSON to the bus.\nfunc (d *DriftDetector) emit(feature string, p float64, win, base int) {\n\tevt
